target.title: feat(msteams): Teams voice (CVI) + chat + governance integration
target.body:
> **Stacked on #91438 ŌĆö review the increment** (`e86198a8d4ŌĆ”a2268576cd`, 47 commits): the foundation is #91438. The full diff includes #91438's commits only until that PR lands; this branch then rebases to exactly the increment. A commitŌåÆfeature map is below.

## Summary

Builds on the `msteams` voice provider (#91438) along six lines:

- **Correctness / security** ŌĆö fixes the realtime **self-answer (echo) bug**, makes the bridge handshake **replay-proof**, enforces group etiquette and **verbal interrupts deterministically** (in code at the audio egress, not model-trusted), and fixes outbound call-backs that **spoke to a still-ringing phone** (and the context-bleed that caused).
- **Call UX** ŌĆö verbal interrupts ("stop" / wake-phrase forms, **English and Arabic**), notify call-backs (deliver ŌåÆ hang up, un-clipped), **greet-by-name** from the roster, **bilingual Arabic/English**, **DTMF / IVR** ("press 1 toŌĆ”").
- **CVI** ŌĆö retroactive vision ("what did the earlier slide say?"), scene-change + camera/screen ambient vision, captioned/slideshow visual sharing as a **picture-in-picture overlay** over the avatar, a **"thinking"** expression, per-thread sessions.
- **Meeting productivity** ŌĆö opt-in **end-of-meeting minutes** posted to the Teams chat/thread, on-demand **"summarize the meeting"** mid-call, a **Word-openable minutes document**, and **per-person attribution** from unmixed meeting audio.
- **Chat** ŌĆö a Teams **"Ask OpenClaw about this" message action** routes a selected chat message to the agent; opt-in **voice-message transcription** folds spoken clips into the agent's turn.
- **Governance** ŌĆö opt-in **audit-log channel** mirroring agent replies and **DLP-aware outbound redaction**.

A post-feature **self-review hardening pass** then audited the whole increment and fixed everything it found (2 critical, 13 bugs, 1 security finding, structural refactors) ŌĆö see "Review hardening" below.

| Layer | Themes |
| --- | --- |
| **Correctness / security** | echo self-answer fix (`suppressInputDuringPlayback` ŌåÆ on), replay-proof HMAC tuples, deterministic group gate on realtime, playout clock for echo + notify drain, **greet-on-answer** (outbound call-backs no longer deliver to a ringing phone) |
| **Call UX** | verbal interrupts (EN + AR), notify call-backs (deliver ŌåÆ hang up), **roster greeting by name**, **bilingual Arabic/English**, **DTMF / IVR** |
| **CVI** | retroactive vision (16-keyframe ring), scene-change + camera/screen vision, captions + slideshow, **PiP overlay**, "thinking" expression, per-thread sessions |
| **Meeting productivity** | end-of-meeting recap to chat/thread, on-demand `/summarize`, DOCX minutes artifact, per-speaker attribution (unmixed audio) |
| **Chat** | "Ask OpenClaw about this" message action, voice-message transcription |
| **Governance** | audit-log channel, DLP outbound redaction |
| **Review hardening** | single per-call cleanup owner, deliver-to-ringing (streaming), un-hangup-able call, 10 more bugs, `withConsultGuards` + structural refactors |

## ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Behavior changes

- `realtime.suppressInputDuringPlayback` default **flipped off ŌåÆ on**: default-off *was* the live self-answer bug ŌĆö the bot answered its own echo on every realtime deployment. Set `false` to restore; tune via `realtime.echoSuppressionWindowMs` / `realtime.echoBargeInRms`.
- Outbound call-backs now honor `outbound.defaultMode` (default **notify** = state the result, then hang up after the audio drains) **and speak only once the callee answers** (recording-active), not when the bridge connects ŌĆö on **both** the realtime and streaming paths. Previously they greeted on connect ŌĆö i.e. while the phone was still ringing ŌĆö so the result was lost and the idle model could surface unrelated context. Set `"conversation"` to keep the lingering behavior.
- `msteams.meetingRecap` (default **off**) ŌĆö when on, the bot posts meeting minutes to the caller's Teams chat (1:1) or the meeting thread (group) at call end. The bot posting unprompted is gated behind this opt-in.
- `msteams.bilingual` (default **off**) ŌĆö when on, the realtime model is pinned to detect/mirror ArabicŌåöEnglish and translate on request. The deterministic layers (verbal interrupts, visemes) understand Arabic regardless.
- `channels.msteams.transcribeVoiceMessages` (default **off**) ŌĆö when on, inbound Teams voice clips are transcribed (STT cost) and folded into the agent's message text.
- `channels.msteams.auditChannel` (unset by default) ŌĆö when set to a conversation id, agent replies are mirrored there for compliance review.
- `channels.msteams.dlp.enabled` (default **off**) ŌĆö when on, built-in categories and `customPatterns` are redacted from **every** outbound surface (block messages, streamed replies, edits, adaptive cards). Because per-token streaming cannot be redacted retroactively (a secret completing across chunk boundaries would already be on screen), DLP also downgrades `streaming.mode: "partial"` to `"progress"`: the live card shows working status and the redacted full reply lands on it in place.

## ­¤øĪ’ĖÅ Security & correctness hardening

- **Single-use HMAC upgrade tuples (replay guard)** ŌĆö a captured handshake replayed within the HMAC window could open a ghost session after the live call ended. `(callId, timestamp, signature)` tuples are single-use; a legitimate reconnect carries a fresh timestamp; only verified tuples are recorded (no unauthenticated map growth) and entries expire with the **timestamp's own validity window** (a future-dated handshake from worker clock skew can't outlive its record). Tested: connect OK ŌåÆ exact replay 401 ŌåÆ fresh-timestamp reconnect OK.
- **Deterministic group-call gate (realtime)** ŌĆö every finished caller turn is evaluated with the same `shouldRespondToGroupTurn` core the streaming path uses; an unaddressed meeting turn's reply **audio is dropped at the egress**. 1:1 never gates.
- **Echo guard on a playout clock** ŌĆö the realtime model streams faster than realtime, so last-send time is not play-time; the guard and the notify drain both key on a playout estimate (accumulated chunk durations, collapsed on barge-in). Regression test: burst 10 s of audio ŌåÆ echo still suppressed at +2 s.
- **Greet-on-answer (outbound call-backs)** ŌĆö the greeting (the delivered result) now fires on recording-active (the callee answered), not on bridge-connect; this both delivers the result at the right time and closes the idle window where the model would otherwise free-associate from its context.

## ­¤ō× Call experience

- **Deterministic verbal interrupts** ŌĆö "stop" / "wait" / "hold on" / "never mind" handled in code: streaming cuts playback **and suppresses the reply**; realtime flushes the worker queue. Whole-utterance match with filler **and configured wake-phrase stripping** ŌĆö *"Ō¤©nameŌ¤®, stop"* cuts as instantly as bare *"stop"*; *"stop by the store"* never triggers. **Unicode-aware**: Arabic interrupts ("ž¬┘ł┘é┘ü", "ž«┘äž¦žĄ", ŌĆ”) cut identically, with tashkeel-safe normalization (same `\p{L}` approach as the group gate).
- **Roster greeting** ŌĆö the model greets the caller by first name (from the Teams roster) and addresses participants by the per-utterance speaker label.
- **Bilingual Arabic/English** (opt-in) ŌĆö detect, mirror, switch-with-caller, translate on request; the viseme estimator also maps Arabic graphemes so lip-sync isn't RMS-only in Arabic.
- **DTMF / IVR** ŌĆö in-band keypad tones from the caller are surfaced to the realtime model as a user message ("the caller pressed 1"), so the agent can run "press 1 toŌĆ”" flows. *(Worker companion forwards the tone.)*
- **Notify call-backs deliver-and-hang-up without clipping** ŌĆö drain-aware (playout clock) + `notifyHangupDelaySec` grace; a failed hangup falls back to a direct teardown.

## ­¤Äź CVI extensions

- **Retroactive vision (frame history)** ŌĆö a ring of 16 scene-change keyframes per call; `look_at_screen scope:"history"` attaches up to 6 timestamped, attributed keyframes to **one budgeted consult**. No eager OCR-per-keyframe.
- **Scene-change-driven ambient vision** ŌĆö frames reach the realtime model on arrival; **camera + screen simultaneously**, per-source dedup + labels.
- **`show_to_caller` captions + slideshow + PiP overlay** ŌĆö caption strip on the tile; multi-image results play as a paced slideshow; images ride as a **bottom-right inset over the live avatar** (`display.image mode:"overlay"`) so the bot stays visible. Additive protocol; older renderers fall back to fullscreen.
- **"Thinking" avatar expression** while a tool runs; the reply re-cues the real emotion.
- **Real lip-sync timing** ŌĆö the elevenlabs provider's telephony synthesis uses `/with-timestamps` and surfaces per-character alignment (optional field through `textToSpeechTelephony`); msteams `speech.marks` now carry each sound's *actual* start time instead of an even spread, falling back to the estimator when alignment is absent. Audio never depends on timing: a failing `/with-timestamps` endpoint degrades to plain synthesis.
- **Per-thread sessions** ŌĆö `sessionScope: "per-thread"` keys the agent session by the Teams meeting/chat thread; parallel conversations stay isolated. PSTN falls through to per-phone.

## ­¤ōØ Meeting productivity

- **End-of-meeting recap** (opt-in `msteams.meetingRecap`) ŌĆö key points / decisions / action items posted to the caller's Teams chat (1:1) or **into the meeting thread** (group) when the call ends.
- **On-demand minutes** ŌĆö "summarize the meeting" mid-call posts the running minutes to chat without ending the call (`post_meeting_minutes` tool).
- **Word-openable minutes artifact** ŌĆö the recap attaches a formatted document the caller can open in Word.
- **Per-person attribution** ŌĆö with unmixed meeting audio the worker tags each turn with the speaker, so minutes and transcripts read "Sara: ŌĆ”" instead of one mixed stream.

## ­¤Æ¼ Chat & governance

- **"Ask OpenClaw about this" message action** ŌĆö a Teams message-context command (`composeExtension/submitAction`) quotes the selected chat message into a prompt and dispatches it through the normal message path, so the answer posts back into the conversation. *(Requires the Teams app manifest to declare the command: `composeExtensions > commands`, `type: "action"`, `context: ["message"]`, `id: "askOpenClaw"`.)*
- **Voice messages** (opt-in `transcribeVoiceMessages`) ŌĆö inbound Teams audio clips are transcribed and folded into the agent's turn as a quoted block; a clip that fails transcription keeps its attachment placeholder.
- **Audit-log channel** (opt-in `auditChannel`) ŌĆö agent replies are mirrored to a named Teams conversation for compliance review. The mirror fires at the **delivery choke point**, so natively-streamed replies (the DM default) are in the trail too, not only block deliveries; the loop guard keeps the audit channel's own traffic out.
- **DLP outbound redaction** (opt-in `dlp.enabled`) ŌĆö built-in categories plus custom patterns are redacted with a configurable placeholder, on **every** outbound surface: block messages, streamed replies (see Behavior changes), message **edits**, and **adaptive cards** (deep-redacted per string value). The audit excerpt is redacted **before** truncation so the cap can never slice a secret into an unrecognizable fragment.

## ­¤öÄ Review hardening (post-feature self-audit)

After the feature work, a full self-review of the increment found and fixed ŌĆö each with a regression test:

- **2 critical** ŌĆö realtime mode never wired the CallManager (the stale-call reaper force-ended connected outbound callbacks ~2 min in); the msteams safe-allowlist inbound default was dead code (msteams+streaming rejected ALL inbound calls in production config). (`39712c61ed`)
- **1 security** ŌĆö HMAC replay records now expire at `ts+window`, not `now+window`, closing a replay window for future-dated (clock-skewed) handshakes. (`cc9922bbe4`)
- **4 state-leak paths, one design fix** ŌĆö a single `releaseCallState` owner that every teardown funnels through: host-initiated closes no longer suppress `onSessionEnd` (dead calls reported in-progress forever); a realtime provider WS drop no longer strands the caller on an **un-hangup-able call**; manager hangups release vision/recording state (~1ŌĆō2 MB/call); a declined outbound call no longer leaves its CallRecord active against `maxConcurrentCalls`. (`35266045a8`)
- **9 individual bugs** ŌĆö deliver-to-ringing on the *streaming* path; custom-tool calls stalling the model turn; unbounded/misattributed transcript coalescing; the message action being mention-gated to silence in group chats; history-look poisoning the live vision cache; the minutes tool skipping the recording gate + the task tool acking a no-op; empty-`aadId` cross-caller session bleed; lost-and-budget-burning ambient vision pushes; voice-clip placeholder mishandling. (`9e16ca20a6`)
- **Structural refactors** so this class of bug has fewer places to hide ŌĆö `withConsultGuards` (one copy of the recording-gate/deps/error/filler boilerplate for all agent-running tools), `emitCallEnded`, a single hoisted consult session key, the tool surface split into `msteams-realtime-tools.ts`, and the msteams runtime no longer building the webhook-plane realtime handler Teams never uses. (`4fe8cd1e08`)
- **Bilingual honored by the deterministic layers** ŌĆö verbal interrupts match Arabic (Unicode normalization + Arabic phrase set) and the viseme map covers Arabic graphemes. (`828bb566ff`)
- **4 governance bypasses (the review's most severe findings)** ŌĆö DLP/audit only covered the block-delivery text path. Now: native streaming redacted (token streaming downgrades to progress under DLP ŌĆö append-only streams can't retract a secret that completes across chunks); the audit mirror moved to the delivery choke point so streamed replies are in the trail; message edits and adaptive cards redact (closing the "edit the secret in" / "put it in a card" loopholes); the secret pattern catches segmented `sk-proj-`/`sk-ant-` keys and the audit excerpt redacts before truncating. (`a8381d3002`)
- **Poll-card DLP redaction (Codex review follow-up)** ŌĆö the ClawSweeper Codex pass caught a remaining card surface: `sendPollMSTeams` sent the poll Adaptive Card unredacted (the S3 fix only covered the generic adaptive-card path), so a secret in a poll question or option label reached the wire. The poll's agent-supplied question and option strings are now redacted before the card is built ŌĆö visible text only, leaving the internal option indices and `pollId` intact so vote matching is unaffected. (`1629166d82`)

## Commit ŌåÆ feature map (increment beyond #91438)

| Feature | Commits |
| --- | --- |
| Refactors (vision store, tts playback) | `e86198a8d4`, `3b4a728dfa` |
| Echo / self-answer fix (+ knobs, playout clock) | `7dbd986df4`, `92867d4c78`, `b3a9252758` |
| Captions + slideshow | `fd6c82cf6d`, `5bfb0ca698` |
| Notify call-backs (mode + drain + greet-on-answer) | `27ee822939`, `619134b702`, `7cb6851fac` |
| Scene-change + camera/screen vision | `3cfe55c4be`, `898aed97c9` |
| "Thinking" expression | `2a97fa0507` |
| Replay guard (+ ts-window expiry) | `4b2f9a6ec9`, `cc9922bbe4` |
| Deterministic group gate | `cfd5263da9` |
| Retroactive vision (keyframe ring) | `f0ced39f66` |
| Per-thread sessions | `aa59abc7ab` |
| Verbal interrupts (+ wake-phrase form, + Arabic) | `83765be05f`, `d3f7f43c80`, `828bb566ff` |
| Meeting recap + thread + on-demand + DOCX + attribution | `4f093d6342`, `77468b7b07`, `d0d5b75ea0`, `d8e737b709`, `5a4dee03fe`, `12abf0cc89` |
| PiP overlay (#17) | `ca32343032` |
| Roster greeting (#20) | `8d7d6ba180` |
| Bilingual (#19) | `c427f0244e`, `828bb566ff` |
| DTMF / IVR (#21) | `7d9731d179` |
| "Ask OpenClaw about this" message action (#10) | `f93e8a179a` |

| DLP outbound redaction (#16) | `a12a3844c8` |
| Audit-log channel (#15) | `8d86842aee`, `661fc2d2fa` |
| Review hardening (critical / security / bugs / refactors) | `39712c61ed`, `d6cea6b313`, `35266045a8`, `9e16ca20a6`, `4fe8cd1e08` |
| Governance bypass closure (streaming/edit/card DLP + audit choke point) | `a8381d3002` |
| Regenerated bundled channel metadata (governance keys were unconfigurable) | `283655fd9c` |
| Real viseme timing (ElevenLabs with-timestamps alignment) | `a2268576cd` |
| Poll-card DLP redaction (Codex review follow-up) | `1629166d82` |

Each feature ships with unit tests (matcher/positive-negative units, display-path, history-ring, group-gate egress drop, replay 401/reconnect, recap prompt, DTMF mapping, message-action extraction, teardown/leak regressions, Arabic interrupt/viseme cases, etc.).

## Ō£ģ Live validation (2026-06-12, real Teams calls)

Re-validated end-to-end on this branch's exact head against a production Teams tenant (gateway from source, companion media worker as a Windows service), in two calls:

- **Inbound 1:1 call (~7.5 min)** ŌĆö roster greeting by name; no echo self-answer with the reply on open speakers; barge-in; deterministic verbal interrupts in **English, wake-phrase form, and Arabic ("ž¬┘ł┘é┘ü")**; bilingual ArabicŌåöEnglish mirroring + on-request translation; `openclaw_agent_consult` with the **"thinking" avatar expression** held across six tool runs (worker log); emotion cues (Happy/Neutral transitions) tracking reply tone; **screen-share vision** (`look_at_screen`, scene-change awareness, retroactive keyframe history ŌĆö two VBSS share windows in the worker log); **`show_to_caller`** rendered two images as a **PiP overlay with caption**, paced as a slideshow with exactly the coded hold times (4500 ms non-final / 5000 ms final).
- **Outbound notify call-back** (placed by an in-call background task): `outbound call placed ŌåÆ Established ŌåÆ recording-active gate opened ŌåÆ result spoken only after answer (greet-on-answer) ŌåÆ notify-mode auto-hangup after the audio drained` ŌĆö complete, un-clipped delivery, clean teardown, no leaked call state.
- **Meeting productivity** ŌĆö on-hangup recap fired (`posting meeting recap`) and the minutes landed in the Teams chat; in-call background task delivered its answer to chat.
- Both calls tore down cleanly (`connection closed` with no lingering state) ŌĆö the review-hardening teardown paths behaving live.
- The validation also caught that the **governance keys were unconfigurable** (stale generated channel metadata + a Windows-silent regen script) ŌĆö fixed in `283655fd9c`; the gateway now starts with `dlp`/`transcribeVoiceMessages`/`auditChannel` configured.

**Chat & governance (same day, follow-up session):**

- **Audit mirror on streamed replies (S2)** ŌĆö with `auditChannel` set to a dedicated group chat, every streamed DM reply produced a `­¤¦Š Reply for <sender> in <conversation>: <excerpt>` line in the audit chat; the bot's own reply *inside* the audit chat produced no self-mirror (loop guard).
- **DLP on the wire (S1)** ŌĆö a DM reply containing an email rendered as `ŌĆ”([REDACTED:email])` in the Teams client: the model emitted the address, the redaction layer scrubbed it on the **streamed** path ŌĆö the exact bypass this PR closes. (The `secret` category never reached the wire live because the model itself refused to repeat even a dummy `sk-proj-ŌĆ”` token ŌĆö twice; the category patterns are unit-tested and the wire path is category-agnostic.)
- **Message action (#10)** ŌĆö *Ask OpenClaw about this* on a DM message dispatched the quoted-message prompt and produced a substantive reply in the conversation. (The group/mention-gate case is unit-tested.)
- **Voice-message transcription (#13)** ŌĆö not live-testable in this tenant: Teams desktop's mic-only "video clip" is a stream attachment that delivers **no media to bots** (the bot correctly reported only the text label arrived), and the tenant's mobile client has no voice-clip button. Unit-tested; the graceful no-media handling was observed live.
- **Mention gating** ŌĆö unmentioned messages in the group chat were correctly ignored; a mentioned message was answered.

**Real lip-sync timing (2026-06-13, follow-up):** the ElevenLabs `/with-timestamps` alignment path (`a2268576cd`) was validated on a live Teams call with the SVG face avatar and debug logging. The greeting's mouth track was driven by real per-character timing, not the even-spread estimator:

```text
04:53:23 MsteamsProvider: session.start callId=22005380-ŌĆ”
04:53:24 MsteamsProvider: recording.status 22005380-ŌĆ” = active
04:53:2x MsteamsProvider: expression cue 'neutral' for 22005380-ŌĆ”        ŌåÉ debug level active
04:53:2x MsteamsProvider: speech.marks 60 visemes (aligned) for 22005380-ŌĆ”   ŌåÉ real ElevenLabs alignment
04:53:36 [worker] Call status updated to Terminated - The conversation has ended
04:53:36 [worker] OpenClaw bridge closed for call 22005380-ŌĆ” (call-ended); disposed 0 audioMediaBuffers
```

`(aligned)`, not `(estimated)` ŌĆö the with-timestamps endpoint succeeded with no fallback, and the 60 marks carry each character's actual start time. The caller-hangup teardown was clean on both sides (Graph `Terminated` ŌåÆ bridge closed ŌåÆ disposed, no leak). Full evidence: `docs/proofs/LIVE-TEST-92081.md` in the companion worker repo.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** End-to-end behavior of this PR on real Microsoft Teams calls and chats: realtime voice (echo self-answer fix, deterministic verbal interrupts incl. Arabic, roster greeting, bilingual), CVI vision (`look_at_screen`, scene-change ambient push, retroactive history, `show_to_caller` PiP/caption/slideshow), outbound notify call-back (greet-on-answer, drain-aware auto-hangup), meeting minutes, and governance (audit-channel mirror on streamed replies, DLP redaction on the wire).
- **Real environment tested:** Production Microsoft 365 tenant (PCFC, Dubai) with the Teams desktop client; OpenClaw gateway run from source at this branch's head on a Windows Server VM; the companion app-hosted media worker running as a Windows service; Azure OpenAI realtime voice model; ElevenLabs STT/TTS.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm openclaw gateway run` on the branch head with `msteams.dlp.enabled`, `auditChannel`, `meetingRecap`, and `bilingual` configured ŌåÆ placed a 1:1 Teams voice call to the bot and exercised greeting, echo, interrupts ("stop" / "assistant, stop" / "ž¬┘ł┘é┘ü"), Arabic conversation, screen-share vision, "show me a screenshot", and "summarize the meeting" ŌåÆ said "look up the time in Tokyo and call me back", hung up, and answered the bot's call-back ŌåÆ ran the chat pass: DM questions including "reply with exactly: contact our support team at a.b@example.com", with an "Audit Log" group chat configured as the audit channel.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied (redacted) gateway runtime log from the live calls:

  ```text
  06:18:54 MsteamsProvider: realtime session.start callId=24005480-ŌĆ” caller.aadId=9a78ŌĆ”
  06:18:54 MsteamsProvider: recording.status 24005480-ŌĆ” = active (realtime)
  06:26:22 MsteamsRealtime: posting meeting recap for 24005480-ŌĆ”
  06:26:32 MsteamsProvider: outbound call placed callId=2b005180-ŌĆ” -> 9a78ŌĆ”   (the caller)
  06:26:34 MsteamsProvider: outbound realtime session.start ŌĆ” attached to internal e4adŌĆ”
  06:26:38 MsteamsProvider: recording.status 2b005180-ŌĆ” = active (realtime)  ŌåÉ greet-on-ANSWER trigger
  06:26:52 MsteamsProvider: notify-mode auto-hangup for 2b005180-ŌĆ” after delivery
  ```

  Worker runtime log excerpt: `[display] showing image for 4500 ms (mode=overlay, caption=True)` followed by `5000 ms` ŌĆö the `show_to_caller` slideshow pacing constants observed on the wire. Copied live output from the Teams client: the bot's streamed DM reply rendered as `Noted ŌĆö thanks, Alaaeldin. I've got the support contact ([REDACTED:email]).`, and the audit chat received `­¤¦Š Reply for Eng. Alaaeldin Mohamed Elhenawy in a:180GtŌĆ”: ŌĆ”` mirror lines ŌĆö one per streamed DM reply.
- **Observed result after fix:** Caller greeted by name; the bot never answered its own echo; all three interrupt forms cut playback instantly; vision answered correctly across two screen-share windows including "what did the FIRST screen say"; the captioned PiP slideshow displayed; the call-back spoke only after the callee answered, delivered the complete un-clipped result, and hung itself up; recap + minutes landed in the Teams chat; the email in a reply reached the client as `[REDACTED:email]`; every streamed DM reply was mirrored to the audit channel and the bot's own reply inside the audit chat produced no self-mirror.
- **What was not tested:** Voice-message transcription live (this tenant exposes no audio voice-message capability on either Teams client ŌĆö the desktop mic-only "video clip" delivers no media to bots, and the bot's graceful no-media reply WAS observed live); the DLP `secret` category on the wire (the model refused to repeat even a stated-dummy `sk-proj-ŌĆ”` token, twice ŌĆö the email category proves the same redaction path); the group-chat form of the message action; DTMF (no dialpad on VoIP 1:1 calls); group/meeting calls with a second human participant.

## Foundation recap (from #91438, included in this diff until it lands)

The `msteams` provider: inbound Teams voice + video vision (`look_at_screen`, streaming auto-attach, realtime continuous vision via `RealtimeVoiceBridge.sendImage`), group/meeting awareness (per-participant attribution + speak-only-when-addressed), outbound "call me back" with voicemail fallback, avatar rendering drivers (expression cues, viseme lip-shape, `show_to_caller`), caller allowlist (closed by default), recording-status gate on every media-derived path, HMAC-authenticated bridge WebSocket, SSRF-guarded outbound. Live-proven on 1:1 and 3-party group Teams calls.

## Notes
- Several features (DTMF tone capture, unmixed meeting audio, avatar rendering) have a companion in the application-hosted media worker; the protocol additions are backward-compatible (older workers ignore unknown fields).
- The avatar **art** is a worker-side choice (not an OpenClaw config key): the companion ships two styles ŌĆö an SVG face with full lip-sync/viseme shapes, expressions, and blink (`AvatarImagePath=avatar`, the default), and a Lottie loop with speech-reactive zoom (`AvatarImagePath=jarvis`) ŌĆö plus explicit file paths and a procedural fallback. The `expression` / `speech.marks` / `display.image` messages this PR emits drive whichever style is configured.
- The recap/minutes, bilingual, voice-transcription, audit, and DLP features are **opt-in**; defaults preserve prior behavior except the two flagged behavior changes above (echo guard, greet-on-answer).


## Acknowledgements

Contributed by the team at the Ports, Customs and Free Zone Corporation (PCFC) ŌĆö Dubai, United Arab Emirates ŌĆö bringing Microsoft Teams voice & video (the Conversational Video Interface) to OpenClaw for public-sector use. We're glad to share it upstream and welcome the maintainers' guidance on shaping it for the wider community.

target.title: fix(config): refuse to restore from a polluted backup containing redacted secrets (#68423)
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #68423.

`maybeRecoverSuspiciousConfigRead` would silently restore from `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak` whenever the live config looked clobbered (update-channel-only root). It did not verify the backup itself was clean. If an agent had previously written tool-output redactions (`"***"` / `"<short>...<short>"`) back into the live config, the rotation pipeline copied that pollution to `.bak`, and the next observe pass _restored_ from the polluted backup ŌĆö fixating the pollution. The reporter hit a 14-hour 401 outage from this on 2026-04-18.

## Change

- `src/config/backup-pollution.ts` (new): `findRedactedSecretSites(parsed)` walks an arbitrary parsed config and returns dot-paths of secret-shaped fields (apiKey, token, secret, password, key, credential) whose value matches one of:
  - `***` or longer asterisk runs (Hermes-style redaction)
  - `<short prefix>...<short suffix>` ŌĆö OpenClaw maskApiKey output shape, bounded so high-entropy real secrets containing dots don't false-positive
  - `<short prefix>ŌĆ”<short suffix>` ŌĆö unicode-ellipsis variant
- `src/config/io.observe-recovery.ts`: both async + sync recovery paths now call `findRedactedSecretSites` on the parsed backup before copying it over the live config. If any sites match, the recovery is refused, a warn line lists the offending paths, and the original (clobbered) raw is returned so the gateway surfaces the real failure rather than fixating pollution.

## Why this scope only

This PR addresses the most acute item from the issue (refuse restore from polluted backup). The reporter also suggested:

1. Validating before _creating_ backups (prevent pollution spread)
2. Multi-level rotation (`.bak.daily`, `.bak.weekly`)
3. `openclaw config validate` CLI

Those are valuable but each touches a different code path (`maintainConfigBackups`, rotation policy, CLI surface). Easier to land separately once the maintainer signals direction. Happy to follow up on any of them.

## Tests

- `src/config/backup-pollution.test.ts` ŌĆö 8 unit tests covering clean configs, asterisk + ASCII ellipsis + unicode ellipsis, multi-site detection, non-secret keys ignored, long real secrets ignored, array walking.
- `src/config/io.observe-recovery.test.ts` ŌĆö new integration test seeds a polluted `.bak` (`gateway.auth.token: "***"`) plus a clobbered live config and asserts the recovery is refused and the warn line names `gateway.auth.token`.

\`pnpm vitest run src/config/backup-pollution.test.ts src/config/io.observe-recovery.test.ts\` ŌĆö 12/12 passing locally.

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests for `findRedactedSecretSites` cover the three redaction shapes
- [x] Integration test covers the refusal path on the async recovery
- [x] Adjacent config tests still pass (`io.write-config.test.ts`, `config.backup-rotation.test.ts`)

target.title: fix(discord): record history entry when dropping bot message in allowBots=mentions mode
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** When `allowBots=mentions` is configured on a Discord account, bot messages that are dropped (no mention) skip `recordPendingHistoryEntryIfEnabled()`, causing gaps in guild channel history. Other bots in the same channel lose context about dropped messages.
- **Why it matters:** In multi-bot setups, history continuity is critical for context-aware responses. Missing history entries cause bots to "forget" recent bot messages in the channel.
- **What changed:** Added `recordPendingHistoryEntryIfEnabled()` call in the `allowBots=mentions` drop path before `return null`, matching the pattern already used in the `requireMention` drop path.
- **What did NOT change:** No logic change to when messages are dropped; only ensures history is recorded before dropping.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: The `allowBots=mentions` drop path was added without the `recordPendingHistoryEntryIfEnabled()` call that other drop paths already have.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for history recording in the `allowBots=mentions` drop path.
- Prior context: The `requireMention` drop path at line ~900 already records history before returning null.
- Why this regressed now: The `allowBots` feature was added after the history recording pattern was established.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.preflight.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: When a bot message is dropped due to missing mention in `allowBots=mentions` mode, the guild history map should still contain the dropped message entry.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Unit test directly verifies the history map state after the drop.
- Existing test that already covers this: None previously; added in this PR.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

In multi-bot Discord setups with `allowBots=mentions`, bot messages that are not directly mentioned are now properly recorded in channel history, improving context continuity for other bots.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[bot message, no @mention] -> drop (history NOT recorded) -> other bots lose context

After:
[bot message, no @mention] -> record history -> drop -> other bots retain context
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime: Node.js 22
- Integration/channel: Discord (multi-bot gateway, 6 bots)

### Steps

1. Configure 2+ Discord bot accounts with `allowBots: "mentions"`
2. Bot A sends a message in a shared channel without @mentioning Bot B
3. Bot B drops the message (correct behavior)
4. Check Bot B's guild history for the channel

### Expected

Bot B's guild history contains Bot A's dropped message.

### Actual

Before fix: history entry missing. After fix: history entry recorded.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- Test: `pnpm test -- extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.preflight.test.ts` ŌĆö "records history when dropping bot message without mention (allowBots=mentions)" passes.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Bot message drop with history recording, bot message with mention (still passes through)
- Edge cases checked: PluralKit bots (exempt from allowBots gate), DM messages (no history recording needed)
- What you did **not** verify: History playback in actual LLM responses across bots

target.title: feat(memory-lancedb): support reindex and drop-index command
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `ltm reindex` rebuilt the LanceDB vector index but did not wait for index creation to finish before reporting success.
- Why it matters: the command could print a successful result while the index was still building in the background, which is misleading for maintenance workflows.
- What changed: added a `--timeout <seconds>` option to `ltm reindex` with a default of `180`, and passed that value into LanceDB via `waitTimeoutSeconds` so reindex waits for completion up to the configured timeout.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not change memory capture/recall behavior, embedding generation, search scoring, or `drop-index` cleanup semantics.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: the CLI surface for `ltm reindex` now accepts a timeout parameter, but it only affects waiting behavior for an existing maintenance command and does not expand data access or privilege boundaries.

target.title: feat(providers/zai): inject X-Session-Id header for prompt cache stickiness
target.body: ## Summary Add `X-Session-Id` header injection for the `zai` provider to enable sticky routing on ZhipuAI's backend load balancer. On a live agent workload (78 GLM-5.1 calls in a single session), this lifts prompt-cache hit rate from **~52% ŌåÆ ~96%** without any user-facing configuration change. Per ZhipuAI support team guidance, sending a stable `X-Session-Id` header per logical session causes the backend LB to route all requests in that session to the same inference node ŌĆö which aligns prompt-cache residency with the user's actual session boundaries instead of letting LB variance thrash the cache. ## Motivation On tool-heavy multi-turn agent workloads, the system prompt and tool definitions dominate the prompt tokens and remain stable across calls, so they *should* live in the provider's prompt cache. But without a stable session key, ZhipuAI's backend LB may route successive calls from the same logical session to different inference nodes, each of which has to rebuild the prefix cache independently. The observable symptom is a sawtooth per-call hit-rate pattern that plateaus far below the ceiling. This PR mirrors the same "header wrapper" shape already in use for the `xai` provider (`extensions/xai/stream.ts`), so the change is idiomatic and low-risk. ## Design Added `extensions/zai/stream.ts`: - **`createZaiSessionIdHeaderWrapper(baseStreamFn?)`** ŌĆö stream wrapper that reads `options.sessionId`, validates it, and injects `X-Session-Id` into the outbound headers. When `sessionId` is missing, empty, whitespace-only, or exceeds 256 chars, the wrapper passes through unchanged (no header injected). - **`wrapZaiProviderStream(ctx)`** ŌĆö provider-level composition. Composes the session-id header wrapper with the existing `tool-stream-default-on` behavior via `composeProviderStreamWrappers` + `createToolStreamWrapper`. Shape mirrors `extensions/xai/stream.ts`. `extensions/zai/index.ts` now registers the provider with `wrapStreamFn: wrapZaiProviderStream` instead of spreading `ZAI_TOOL_STREAM_HOOKS`, preserving the existing tool-stream behavior while adding the header injection. ## Visual comparison Same task (LinkedIn jobs research, multi-turn browser agent) on GLM-5.1, observed end-to-end: **Before PR** (orange, 50 calls): per-call hit rate is sawtoothed between ~8% and ~99%; cumulative rate plateaus at **52.4%**. 24 of 50 calls land in the "cold" bucket (hit < 10%), indicating routing-driven cache misses recur throughout the session, not just at the start. **After PR** (green, 78 calls): per-call hit rate climbs to 95-99% within the first 2-3 calls and stays essentially flat for the rest of the session; cumulative rate converges to **96.27%**. Only **1** cold call (the mandatory first-call warm-up). Two `├Ś` markers indicate network errors that short-circuited usage accounting ŌĆö unrelated to this change. ## Test plan ### Unit tests (`extensions/zai/stream.test.ts`, 13 tests, all passing) - sessionId happy path (injects `X-Session-Id` into outgoing headers) - sessionId preserves other headers passed in options - sessionId === `null`, `undefined`, empty string, whitespace-only ŌåÆ no header injected - sessionId > 256 chars ŌåÆ no header injected (length guard) - sessionId containing unicode / special characters ŌåÆ preserved verbatim - `wrapZaiProviderStream` composition with `tool_stream: true` and `tool_stream: false` - `createZaiSessionIdHeaderWrapper` with and without explicit `baseStreamFn` ``` Ō£ō extension-providers extensions/zai/stream.test.ts (13 tests) 16ms Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 13 passed (13) ``` The pre-existing `cache-ttl.test.ts` (added in #24541) still passes, confirming no regression on the cache-TTL eligibility path that zai shares with moonshot. ### Live benchmark Identical agent task run under two configurations: | Metric | Before (no X-Session-Id) | After (this PR) | |------------------------------|-------------------------:|----------------:| | Calls (successful / errored) | 50 / 0 | 76 / 2 (network) | | Total prompt tokens | 1,589,843 | 2,672,479 | | Cached (hit) tokens | 832,896 | 2,572,736 | | Uncached (miss) tokens | 756,947 | 99,743 | | **Cache hit rate (aggregate)** | **52.39%** | **96.27%** | | **Median per-call hit rate** | **79.35%** | **99.11%** | | Cold calls (hit < 10%) | 24 / 50 | 1 / 76 | | Unique session IDs | 50 (per-request UUIDs) | 1 (session-level) | ### Cost impact Assuming GLM-5.1 cached tokens bill at ~20% of the input rate, effective input-token cost drops from ~0.58├Ś nominal to ~0.23├Ś nominal on this workload ŌĆö **roughly a 60% input-token cost reduction**. ## Alternatives considered ### Why not rely on pi-ai's `sendSessionAffinityHeaders` compat gate? `pi-ai@0.69.0` ships a generic session-affinity mechanism in `providers/openai-completions.js` that, when `compat.sendSessionAffinityHeaders === true`, injects three headers ŌĆö `session_id`, `x-client-request-id`, and `x-session-affinity` ŌĆö on OpenAI-completions requests. On paper this would cover the zai transport without any plugin-local wrapper. In practice it doesn't help for this PR: 1. **The gate is default-off and currently unreached.** `detectCompat` hardcodes `sendSessionAffinityHeaders: false` for every provider. Neither pi-ai itself nor `openclaw/main` contains a single write that sets it to `true` ŌĆö both `grep -rn 'sendSessionAffinityHeaders:\s*true' node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-ai/dist` and `git grep sendSessionAffinityHeaders origin/main` return no matches. Turning it on for zai would be a separate decision in its own right. 2. **The header names don't match.** The three headers above use snake_case `session_id` plus hyphenated `x-*` variants. ZhipuAI's support team specified `X-Session-Id` (capital X, capital S, capital I, hyphen-separated) as the exact sticky-routing key their load balancer matches on. We can't assume the LB accepts the pi-ai variants. 3. **Scope.** Flipping the compat gate would also change retry, WebSocket warmup, and any other OpenAI-compatible provider that later opts in. Keeping the injection plugin-local via `wrapStreamFn` confines the blast radius to `zai` and to the literal `X-Session-Id` key. If ZhipuAI later confirms they also recognize `session_id` / `x-session-affinity`, flipping the compat gate becomes a reasonable follow-up ŌĆö this PR does not foreclose that path. ## Scope and caveats - This PR changes behavior only for the `zai` provider. No other provider is touched. - The wrapper is a no-op when `sessionId` is not provided, so callers that do not plumb through a session identifier see identical behavior to today. - Baseline data was captured on a deployment that served GLM-5.1 through an OpenAI-compatible proxy layer. The after-PR run is against `api.z.ai` directly using the bundled `zai` provider. Both runs used the same agent, same task, same model, and drew from the same underlying ZhipuAI backend ŌĆö the only variable we deliberately changed is the presence or absence of `X-Session-Id`. - The `X-Session-Id` header semantics are per ZhipuAI support team's prompt-cache best-practice guidance. The provider ignores the header when absent, so no behavior change for upstream-side consumers that don't opt in. ## Related - Follow-up to #24541 (added ZAI/Moonshot to cache-TTL eligible providers). This PR complements the TTL work by addressing the *routing* aspect of prompt-cache optimization. - Related to #24497 (closed) ŌĆö the sticky-routing concern was originally raised there. ## Checklist - [x] Unit tests added (13 new tests) - [x] Existing tests pass (no regression) - [x] CHANGELOG updated - [x] `docs/concepts/model-providers.md` updated - [x] Live-workload benchmark collected and included - [x] Follows existing `extensions/xai/stream.ts` pattern for consistency ## Real behavior proof **Behavior or issue addressed:** On multi-turn GLM-5.1 agent sessions, ZhipuAI's backend load balancer routes successive calls in the same logical session to different inference nodes, so the prompt-prefix cache never stabilizes and per-call hit rate stays low. This PR makes routing sticky by injecting an `X-Session-Id` header derived from the agent `sessionId`. **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw CLI (`feat/zai-session-id-sticky-routing` build, commit `0973c4821e`) on Windows 11, Node 24.13.0, against the live ZhipuAI Open Platform endpoint (`https://open.bigmodel.cn`) using a production GLM-5.1 API key. Workload: a multi-turn browser-driven LinkedIn jobs research agent, identical task and tool definitions across before/after runs. **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** 1. Build the patched CLI from this branch: `pnpm install && pnpm build`. 2. Configure z.ai provider with the real GLM-5.1 key: `openclaw onboard zai` (interactive paste of the live key). 3. Run the same agent task end-to-end before and after: ``` openclaw run --provider zai --model glm-5.1 \ --session-id "linkedin-jobs-research-2026-05-28" \ "research recent ML platform openings on LinkedIn, summarize top 10" ``` 4. Tail `~/.openclaw/logs/.jsonl` for live per-call `usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens` and `usage.prompt_tokens`, captured into the table below. The outbound request headers were also verified live via `openclaw doctor --trace zai` to confirm `X-Session-Id: linkedin-jobs-research-2026-05-28` was actually sent on each call. **Evidence after fix:** Cumulative-cache-hit chart from the live runs (terminal output capture rendered into a side-by-side plot from the redacted runtime usage logs): Redacted live usage tally (copied directly from `openclaw run` stdout, aggregated post-session): ``` [zai/glm-5.1] session=linkedin-jobs-research-2026-05-28 (after fix, 78 calls) total prompt tokens .......... 2,672,479 cached tokens ................ 2,572,736 uncached tokens .............. 99,743 cumulative hit rate .......... 96.27% median per-call hit rate ..... 99.11% cold calls (hit < 10%) ....... 1 X-Session-Id header .......... sent on 78/78 calls (verified via doctor trace) ``` **Observed result after fix:** Per-call prompt-cache hit rate climbs to 95ŌĆō99% within the first 2ŌĆō3 calls and stays essentially flat for the remainder of the session; aggregate hit rate converges to **96.27%** (vs **52.39%** on the unpatched build over an equivalent 50-call run on the same agent task). Only 1 cold call (the mandatory first-turn warm-up). No user-facing config change required; `X-Session-Id` no-ops cleanly for missing/empty/oversize session ids as covered by the unit tests. **What was not tested:** Concurrent multi-process sharing of the same `sessionId` across separate CLI instances (out of scope ŌĆö session ids are per-agent-process). Non-GLM models on the z.ai endpoint were not benchmarked because the routing behavior is GLM-family-specific per ZhipuAI guidance.

target.title: fix(tui): tool events dropped when arriving before chat delta
target.body:
## Problem

When `/verbose full` is set in the TUI, tool execution cards are not rendered for live runs. They only appear after reloading history (e.g. via `/verbose full` which triggers `loadHistory()`). This means the user sees no tool activity during an active agent run.

## Root Cause

In `handleAgentEvent` (`src/tui/tui-event-handlers.ts`), agent events are filtered by an `isKnownRun` check that requires the `runId` to already be registered in `sessionRuns` or `finalizedRuns`. However, `sessionRuns` is only populated in `handleChatEvent` ŌĆö specifically when a `chat` delta arrives.

When an agent starts execution with tool calls (instead of text output), the event arrival order is:

1. `agent` lifecycle start ŌåÆ `isKnownRun=false` ŌåÆ **dropped**
2. `agent` tool start ŌåÆ `isKnownRun=false` ŌåÆ **dropped** (tool card never created)
3. `chat` delta ŌåÆ `noteSessionRun(runId)` ŌåÆ runId now registered
4. Subsequent `agent` tool events ŌåÆ `isKnownRun=true` ŌåÆ rendered

The first tool events are silently discarded, so the tool card is never created and never appears.

History loads work because `loadHistory()` fetches the full message list (including `toolResult` role messages) directly from the Gateway API, bypassing the real-time event stream entirely.

## Fix

Pre-register runIds from the current session in `handleAgentEvent` before the `isKnownRun` check. The Gateway already includes `sessionKey` in agent event payloads, so we can match against the current session to accept legitimate early-arriving events without opening the door to cross-session noise.

Also adds `sessionKey` to the `AgentEvent` type definition since the Gateway payload already includes this field.

## Testing

- All 235 existing TUI tests pass
- All 40 gateway agent-events tests pass
- Pre-commit checks (`pnpm check`) pass
- Manually verified: tool cards now render in real-time with `/verbose full` in the TUI

## AI-Assisted

Yes ŌĆö this PR was identified and fixed with AI assistance. The bug was diagnosed by tracing the event flow through the TUI and Gateway source code.

target.title: fix(mattermost): support filePath and path as mediaUrl fallbacks in handleAction
target.body:
## Summary

The Mattermost `handleAction` only checked `params.media` for media URL, ignoring `filePath` and `path` which are commonly used by agents. This is inconsistent with other channel handlers like Discord and Google Chat.

## Problem

When an agent uses the message tool with `filePath` or `path` instead of `media`:
- Discord handler: supports `media` ŌåÆ `path` ŌåÆ `filePath` fallback
- Google Chat handler: supports `media` ŌåÆ `filePath` ŌåÆ `path` fallback  
- Mattermost handler: only checks `media` ŌåÆ file upload fails silently

## Fix

Add fallback chain for media URL resolution:
1. `params.media`
2. `params.filePath`
3. `params.path`

## Related

This is a partial fix for #67784. The other parts (threadId and mediaLocalRoots) are already addressed by:
- PR #52120 (thread context inheritance)
- PR #58439 (mediaLocalRoots forwarding)

## Test Plan
- [x] Existing unit tests pass
- [x] Lint passes

Closes openclaw#67784 (filePath/path fallback only)

target.title: fix(ci): add post-publish asset verification for macOS stable releases
target.body:
## Summary

- add `verify-release-assets.yml` ŌĆö polls for macOS binaries after a stable GitHub release is published
- add CODEOWNERS entry for `appcast.xml` requiring maintainer review

## Why

v2026.4.12 stable was published at 2026-04-13T12:35:53Z with 0 assets. The beta (v2026.4.12-beta.1) has all 3. The public `macos-release.yml` reported success because it is validation-only ŌĆö the private signing/upload pipeline either was not triggered or did not complete. `appcast.xml` was not updated, so Sparkle reports v2026.4.11 as latest.

No automated check exists to catch this gap between public validation and private asset upload.

```bash
# stable: 0 assets
gh api repos/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tags/v2026.4.12 --jq '.assets | length'
# beta: 3 assets
gh api repos/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tags/v2026.4.12-beta.1 --jq '[.assets[].name]'
# appcast: no entry
curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openclaw/openclaw/main/appcast.xml | grep "2026.4.12"
```

## What changed

**`verify-release-assets.yml`** ŌĆö triggers on `release: published` (stable only) or `workflow_dispatch`. Polls every 60s for up to 45 min for asset count >= 3. Fails the workflow if assets never appear. Warns (does not fail) if `appcast.xml` is not yet updated, since the manual commit may still be pending.

**`.github/CODEOWNERS`** ŌĆö adds `appcast.xml @steipete @vincentkoc @mbelinky`. The Sparkle feed controls what binaries are served to all macOS users.

## What did NOT change

- No changes to `macos-release.yml` or the private pipeline
- No auto-opened issues ŌĆö workflow just fails red, visible in Actions tab
- Does not block or gate the release itself

## Verification

```bash
# validate workflow syntax
actionlint .github/workflows/verify-release-assets.yml

# dispatch against known-bad release (should fail after polling)
gh workflow run verify-release-assets.yml -f tag=v2026.4.12

# dispatch against known-good release (should pass)
gh workflow run verify-release-assets.yml -f tag=v2026.4.11
```

target.title: feat(gateway): infer provider from configured model families
target.body:
Adds a small model-family routing normalization step to `resolveSessionModelRef()` so canonical model names can resolve to the correct configured provider when that mapping is unique.

### What changed
- adds a normalization step on top of `resolveSessionModelRef()`
- infers provider from configured model families when a canonical model name maps uniquely in config
- preserves explicit `providerOverride` selections from the user
- preserves fully-qualified `provider/model` refs without rewriting them
- adds focused tests for canonical-model inference and explicit-provider preservation

### Why
OpenClaw already resolves default/session model refs, but canonical model names like `gpt-5.4` can still rely too heavily on the default provider when the config already makes the intended provider unambiguous.

This PR improves that specific case without redesigning the broader model fallback system.

### Examples
- `modelOverride: "gpt-5.4"` + configured `openai-codex/gpt-5.4`
  ŌåÆ resolves to `openai-codex/gpt-5.4`
- `providerOverride: "openai"` + `modelOverride: "gpt-5.4"`
  ŌåÆ stays on `openai/gpt-5.4`
- `modelOverride: "anthropic/claude-opus-4-1"`
  ŌåÆ preserved as-is

### Tests
- `src/gateway/session-utils.model-family-routing.test.ts`
- `src/gateway/session-utils.search.test.ts`

target.title: fix(agents): release bundle MCP runtime on mid-run session reset
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `resetReplyRunSession` rotated the active `sessionId` after auto-compaction failure, context overflow, or role-ordering conflicts, but it never released the previous session id's entry from the bundle MCP runtime cache.
- Why it matters: `runtimesBySessionId` in `src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts` holds `Client` -> `Transport` -> stdio `ChildProcess` references with no TTL/LRU, so old MCP workers like `gate-mcp`, `bnbchain-mcp`, and `chrome-devtools-mcp` stayed alive and accumulated until memory-constrained deployments OOM'd.
- What changed: `resetReplyRunSession` now disposes the previous session id's bundle MCP runtime in the background with `void ... .catch(...)` after the replacement session is fully established, and logs disposal failures through the existing `deps.error(...)` seam without blocking the retry path.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not alter how the new session is established, and it does not add broader cache eviction or runtime lifecycle changes outside the session-reset path.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #64169
- Related #60656
- Related #62026
- Related #62731
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: mid-run session rotation persisted the new session metadata and optionally deleted the old transcript, but it never released the previous session id from the bundle MCP runtime cache.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the reset-path tests did not assert MCP runtime disposal or cover disposal failures.
- Contributing context (if known): the cache has no TTL/LRU and holds stdio-backed MCP client/process references, so each reset could strand another worker pool.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: resetting a reply run session disposes the previous session runtime, still succeeds when disposal throws, and still deletes the old transcript when `cleanupTranscripts: true` even if disposal fails.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the behavior is owned by `resetReplyRunSession`, and the relevant failure handling is exposed through that helper's dependency seam.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this change.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[reset trigger] -> [new session metadata persisted] -> [old MCP runtime remains cached/alive]

After:
[reset trigger] -> [new session metadata persisted] -> [background dispose of old MCP runtime] -> [retry continues]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (No)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
- New/changed network calls? (No)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
- Data access scope changed? (No)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): MCP stdio runtime path
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Trigger `resetReplyRunSession` from an auto-compaction failure, context overflow, or role-ordering conflict while a bundle MCP runtime is cached for the current session id.
2. Observe that the helper rotates `sessionId`, persists the replacement session metadata, and optionally deletes old transcript files.
3. Verify that the previous session id's MCP runtime is disposed in the background and that disposal failures are logged without preventing the reset from succeeding.

### Expected

- Old session MCP workers are released when the session resets.
- Reset still returns success even if runtime disposal throws.
- Transcript cleanup still occurs when requested.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this change.

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Mid-run reply-session resets should retire the old session id's bundle MCP runtime so the old session runtime cache entry cannot keep stdio MCP workers alive after the reset.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux local OpenClaw source checkout, Node with tsx, using the real bundle MCP runtime manager plus the real `resetReplyRunSession` helper. The probe used a temp session store/transcript under `/tmp` and `mcp.servers: {}` so it could prove the runtime-cache retirement path without exposing private MCP server config.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` against `getOrCreateSessionMcpRuntime`, `getSessionMcpRuntimeManager`, and `resetReplyRunSession`. The command cached `proof-old-session`, invoked a compaction-failure reset with an active session store and follow-up run, then polled `manager.listSessionIds()` for the old session id.
- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
before reset cached sessions: proof-old-session
Config was last written by a newer OpenClaw (2026.5.5); current version is 2026.5.3.
proof reset proof-old-session -> 46b164d1-4cc8-4f2f-9f79-abc9424aac7b
reset returned: true
new active session id differs: true
followup session id updated: true
old session cached after reset: false
cached sessions after reset: (none)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The old session id was present in the real bundle MCP runtime cache before reset and absent after reset; the reset still returned `true`, the active session id rotated, and the follow-up run was rebound to the new session id.
- **What was not tested**: Long-running production RSS over multiple days and teardown of a private third-party stdio MCP server process tree were not exercised in this local probe.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `pnpm tsgo`; `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.test.ts`; `pnpm lint src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.test.ts`; `pnpm format` on the touched files.
- Edge cases checked: disposal success, disposal failure logging, and transcript cleanup when disposal fails.
- What you did **not** verify: long-running production memory behavior outside the targeted reset/test surface.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (Yes)
- Config/env changes? (No)
- Migration needed? (No)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: background disposal could fail or hang independently of the retry path.
  - Mitigation: disposal is intentionally non-blocking, failures are logged through `deps.error(...)`, and tests cover the failure path plus transcript cleanup continuity.

## Tests

- [x] `pnpm tsgo`
- [x] `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.test.ts` (4 passed)
- [x] `pnpm lint src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.test.ts` (0 warnings / 0 errors)
- [x] `pnpm format` on touched files

Expand `parseGoogleChatInboundPayload` to recognise all three payload variants and map to correct event types (MESSAGE, ADDED_TO_SPACE, REMOVED_FROM_SPACE).

target.title: Add workspace-safe temp alias for file tools

target.title: docs(memory): add memory system deep dive guide
target.body:
## Summary

Add a comprehensive memory system deep dive guide, addressing #43032.

### Problem

`docs/concepts/memory.md` covers basics (~600 words) but lacks depth on memory best practices, search optimization, lifecycle management, and advanced patterns.

### New document: `docs/concepts/memory-deep-dive.md` (401 lines)

Covers all 6 sections requested in the issue:

1. **Memory Architecture** ŌĆö Two-layer model (`MEMORY.md` + `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`), indexing scope, what is searchable vs not
2. **Writing Effective Memory** ŌĆö What belongs in MEMORY.md vs daily logs, promotion workflow, formatting tips for better search indexing
3. **Search Optimization** ŌĆö Hybrid retrieval pipeline (keyword + vector + MMR), query strategies (semantic vs exact), `memory_search` ŌåÆ `memory_get` workflow, tuning parameters (`maxResults`, `minScore`)
4. **Memory Maintenance** ŌĆö Heartbeat-based periodic review, cleanup strategies, `heartbeat-state.json` tracking
5. **Advanced Patterns** ŌĆö Entity tracking with stable headings, opinion evolution with timestamps, cross-session recall via `sessionMemory`
6. **Troubleshooting** ŌĆö No results, keyword-only matches, `memory_get` file restrictions, MEMORY.md bloat, missing transcript search

### Navigation

Added to `docs.json` under Sessions and memory ŌåÆ Memory group, right after `concepts/memory`.

### Source references

- `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.ts` ŌĆö default settings (maxResults: 6, maxSnippetChars: 700)
- `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/read-file.ts` ŌĆö `memory_get` file boundary enforcement
- `src/agents/memory-search.ts` ŌĆö search config resolution and sources
- `docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md` ŌĆö existing builtin engine docs

Closes #43032

target.title: fix(telegram): respect topic-level groupPolicy overrides for /commands in forum topics (fixes #56699)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #56699 ŌĆö native commands (`/reset`, `/new`, `/status`) in Telegram forum supergroups were being processed by all bots even when a topic had `groupPolicy: \"disabled\"` configured.

**Root cause:** `resolveTelegramCommandAuth` called `evaluateTelegramGroupPolicyAccess` with `useTopicAndGroupOverrides: false`. This caused the policy resolver to skip `topicConfig.groupPolicy` and `groupConfig.groupPolicy` overrides, falling back to the account-level policy. When a topic was configured with `groupPolicy: \"disabled\"`, that override was ignored for commands but respected for regular messages.

**Fix:** Change `useTopicAndGroupOverrides: false` ŌåÆ `useTopicAndGroupOverrides: true` so native commands use the same topic/group-level policy resolution that regular messages use (`bot-handlers.runtime.ts:586`).

## Test plan

- [x] All 7 existing `bot-native-commands.group-auth.test.ts` tests pass
- [x] All 76 Telegram extension test files pass (1113 tests)
- [x] TypeScript type-check passes
- [x] Manual: in a forum supergroup with `topics["N"].groupPolicy: "disabled"`, `/reset` in topic N should respond with "Telegram group commands are disabled." only for bots that have that topic disabled

­¤ż¢ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(channel-summary): suppress phantom default account when no accounts configured
target.body:
## Problem

When `listAccountIds()` returns an empty array (no Feishu accounts configured), `channel-summary.ts` falls back to `[DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID]` as the resolved account ids. It then calls `buildChannelSummary` with that fallback default account snapshot, producing a spurious "Feishu account default not configured" status line ŌĆö a phantom entry for an account that was never actually configured.

This was reported in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/64707.

## Fix

Two-layer guard in `buildChannelSummary`:

1. **`buildChannelSummary` call**: only invoke when `accountIds.length > 0 || configuredEntries.length > 0` (was: only `configuredEntries.length > 0`)
2. **`lines.push` call**: also guard with `accountIds.length > 0 || configuredEntries.length > 0` to prevent unconditional emission of the status line

The second guard was missing in the initial submission ŌĆö confirmed by [Greptile review](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/64712#issuecomment-4229091834).

## Changes

- `src/infra/channel-summary.ts`: add `hasRealAccounts = accountIds.length > 0 || configuredEntries.length > 0` variable; gate both `buildChannelSummary` and `lines.push` with it
- `src/infra/channel-summary.test.ts`: regression test for the phantom default account case

target.title: fix(macos): prevent menubar icon from freezing when AI providers overload
target.body:
Prevents menubar icon from freezing when AI providers return overload/timeout errors and the gateway never sends run-end events.

**IMPORTANT: This only affects menubar display state. Background jobs continue running normally.**

## Implementation
- **Fast watchdog timer** runs every 30 seconds to update menubar display
- **3-minute eviction threshold** clears stale UI state to prevent frozen menubar  
- **Minimum age protection** (10 seconds) prevents premature eviction of recent activities
- **Job refresh on activity** streaming updates and tool starts refresh display timestamps
- **Role recomputation fix** streaming jobs now properly update role when main session changes
- **MainActor race condition fixes** with proper async/await patterns and error handling
- **Immediate cleanup method** `clearAllActivities()` for gateway disconnection UI cleanup
- **Safe collection pattern** avoids race conditions during UI state updates
- **Proper task lifecycle** with TaskStorage helper for clean cancellation
- **Configurable constants** magic numbers extracted for maintainability

## What This Does NOT Do
- Does not kill or interrupt actual background processes
- Does not stop running shell commands, model requests, or other work
- Only updates the menubar icon state for better user experience

## Philosophy
The menubar should always show current status. If we lose track of job state due to API issues, it's better to show "idle" than a frozen "working" state that never updates.

**Priority: The menubar must NEVER freeze - user experience is #1**

## Testing  
- Comprehensive test coverage with 15 test cases including 6 new edge case tests
- Validates UI state cleanup, role recomputation, timestamp refresh, and race conditions
- Tests minimum age protection, error handling, and MainActor thread safety
- Tests ensure stale display state is cleared to prevent frozen menubar
- All tests pass with proper public API usage

## Fixes Addressed
- Ō£ģ Role recomputation bug in streaming job updates
- Ō£ģ MainActor race conditions in background tasks  
- Ō£ģ Error handling gaps in watchdog loop
- Ō£ģ Missing minimum age protection for eviction
- Ō£ģ Magic numbers extracted to constants
- Ō£ģ Comprehensive test coverage for all scenarios

## Result
Guarantees the menubar never stays frozen while preserving all actual background work.

target.title: Skills: allow symlinked skill dirs in user-managed .agents/skills/
target.body:
## Summary

- **Fix**: `resolveContainedSkillPath` rejects symlinked skill directories inside `~/.agents/skills/` and project `.agents/skills/` because their real (post-resolution) paths live outside the root directory. This is expected for user-managed sources ŌĆö users and external tooling (e.g. `obsidian-wiki setup.sh`) commonly install skills via symlinks pointing elsewhere on disk.
- **Change**: For `agents-skills-personal` and `agents-skills-project` sources, fall back to **logical** (pre-symlink-resolution) path containment when the physical check fails. System-managed sources (managed, bundled) keep the strict physical containment check unchanged.
- Adds 3 test cases: symlinked personal skill, symlinked project skill, and a negative case confirming managed-dir symlinks are still rejected.

Fixes #61585

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test -- src/agents/skills.agents-skills-directory.test.ts` ŌĆö 6/6 pass (3 existing + 3 new)
- [x] `pnpm test -- src/agents/skills` ŌĆö 34/34 pass across all 6 skill test files
- [x] `pnpm tsgo` ŌĆö clean

target.title: Fix ACP image forwarding and Synology invalid-token throttling
target.body:
## Summary

This PR bundles two small, low-risk fixes:

1. Fix ACP attachment collection so URL-only image attachments are forwarded into ACP turns.
2. Fix Synology Chat invalid-token throttling so the lock engages exactly when the configured budget is exhausted.

## What changed

### ACP image forwarding
Fix ACP attachment collection so image attachments that only provide `MediaUrl` are still forwarded into ACP turns. The dispatcher already uses `MediaAttachmentCache`, which supports both local paths and remote URLs, but URL-only images were skipped before buffer loading. This change removes that path-only gate and adds coverage for remote image attachments while preserving the existing safety behavior that blocked local paths do not fall back to remote URLs.

### Synology invalid-token throttling
Fix the Synology Chat invalid-token rate limiter so a source is locked as soon as it exhausts its configured invalid-token budget. The previous comparison used a strict greater-than check, which allowed one extra invalid attempt before returning 429. This change switches the limiter to threshold-based locking and adds a focused regression test for the exact boundary.

## Validation

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts extensions/synology-chat/src/webhook-handler.test.ts`

Result on 2026-05-12 after rebasing onto `origin/main` `5abb4b18c8`:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)  # auto-reply shard
Tests       41 passed (41)

Test Files  1 passed (1)  # extension-messaging shard
Tests       23 passed (23)
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** URL-only ACP image attachments should reach ACP turns, and Synology invalid-token lockout should engage exactly at the configured invalid-token budget without blocking valid zero-budget requests pre-auth.

**Real environment tested:** Local Linux checkout of OpenClaw on branch head `ff7aee2b89`, Node/tsx runtime from the repository dependency set, after rebasing onto `origin/main` `5abb4b18c8`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran one Node/tsx terminal probe importing `resolveAcpAttachments` from `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-attachments.ts`, and a second Node/tsx terminal probe importing `createWebhookHandler` plus Synology test HTTP helpers from `extensions/synology-chat/src`.

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the ACP attachment probe:

```json
{
  "cacheCalls": [
    {
      "url": "https://example.com/inbound.png",
      "maxBytes": 10485760,
      "timeoutMs": 1000
    }
  ],
  "attachments": [
    {
      "mediaType": "image/png",
      "data": "cmVtb3RlLWltYWdl"
    }
  ]
}
```

Terminal output from the Synology webhook probe:

```json
{
  "invalidTokenStatuses": [
    401,
    401,
    401,
    401,
    429
  ],
  "zeroBudgetValidStatus": 204,
  "zeroBudgetDeliveries": 1
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The ACP resolver passed the URL-only image into the attachment cache and emitted an ACP image attachment. The Synology handler returned four 401 responses followed by 429 for a `rateLimitPerMinute: 5` invalid-token sequence, and a valid token on a `rateLimitPerMinute: 0` account returned 204 with one delivered message.

**What was not tested:** No live NAS instance or end-to-end ACP server was used; this proof exercises the exported runtime functions and webhook handler in a local Node process.

target.title: fix(mattermost): forward mediaLocalRoots in handleAction for agent workspace file uploads
target.body:
## Summary

Mattermost's `handleAction` in `extensions/mattermost/src/channel.ts` did not destructure or forward the `mediaLocalRoots` parameter from its context to `sendMessageMattermost`. This caused agent workspace files (under `workspace-<agentId>`) to be silently rejected when sent via the message tool.

## Problem

When an agent uses the **message tool** (`action: "send"`) to send a file from its own workspace directory (e.g., `workspace-mattermost-mot/image.png`) to a Mattermost channel:

1. `executeSendAction` in `outbound-send-service.ts` correctly computes `mediaLocalRoots` (including the agent's workspace directory) and passes it to `dispatchChannelMessageAction`.
2. `dispatchChannelMessageAction` passes the full context (including `mediaLocalRoots`) to the Mattermost plugin's `handleAction`.
3. **Bug**: `handleAction` destructured only `{ action, params, cfg, accountId }` ŌĆö ignoring `mediaLocalRoots`.
4. `sendMessageMattermost` was called without `mediaLocalRoots`, so `loadOutboundMediaFromUrl` fell back to `getDefaultMediaLocalRoots()`, which does **not** include `workspace-<agentId>` directories.
5. `assertLocalMediaAllowed` in `local-media-access.ts` explicitly rejects paths under `workspace-*` when using default roots.
6. The upload error was **silently caught** in `send.ts:428-436`, and the message was sent as plain text without the file attachment ŌĆö no error was surfaced to the agent or user.

Other channel plugins (Discord, Telegram, Google Chat) already correctly destructure and forward `mediaLocalRoots` in their `handleAction` implementations.

## Fix

- Destructure `mediaLocalRoots` from the `handleAction` context parameter.
- Forward it to `sendMessageMattermost` in the send options.

## Verification

**Unit tests:**
- Added a new test case `"forwards mediaLocalRoots on send action with media"` that verifies `mediaLocalRoots` is passed through to `sendMessageMattermost` when `handleAction` processes a send with media.
- All 23 existing Mattermost channel tests continue to pass.

**Manual testing (dev gateway):**
- Configured a Mattermost bot with an agent bound to `workspace-mattermost-mot`.
- On `main` (without fix): agent sends message via message tool with `filePath` pointing to a file in the agent workspace ŌåÆ message arrives as **plain text only**, file attachment is silently dropped.
- On fix branch: same operation ŌåÆ message arrives with the **file attachment correctly uploaded and visible** in Mattermost.

## Files changed

- `extensions/mattermost/src/channel.ts` ŌĆö add `mediaLocalRoots` to `handleAction` destructuring and `sendMessageMattermost` call
- `extensions/mattermost/src/channel.test.ts` ŌĆö add test for `mediaLocalRoots` forwarding in send action

target.title: fix(feishu): parse interactive card post-format fallback content
target.body:
## Summary

Fix `parseInteractiveCardContent()` to handle Feishu API post-format fallback content for interactive card messages.

Fixes #60380
Related: #41609 #48281

## Problem

When reading card messages via `GET /im/v1/messages/{message_id}`, Feishu returns `body.content` in a **post-style fallback format** ŌĆö nested arrays with `tag:"text"` elements ŌĆö instead of the original card template JSON. The existing parser only handled card template format (`div`/`markdown` tags), causing all fallback content to silently return `"[Interactive Card]"`.

**Before:** Any `message(action=read)` on a card ŌåÆ `"[Interactive Card]"`
**After:** Extracts title + text content from both card template and post-format fallback

## Example

API returns this for a card message:
```json
{
  "title": "­¤ż¢ clawdbot",
  "elements": [[
    {"tag": "img", "image_key": "img_v3_02ad_..."},
    {"tag": "text", "text": "Daily report summary"},
    {"tag": "text", "text": "13 people missing reports"}
  ]]
}
```

- **Before:** `"[Interactive Card]"`
- **After:** `"­¤ż¢ clawdbot\nDaily report summary\n13 people missing reports"`

## Changes

**`extensions/feishu/src/send.ts`**
- Handle nested arrays in `elements` (post-format fallback `[[...]]`)
- Extract `tag:"text"` and `tag:"a"` elements from fallback content
- Extract card title from `header.title.content` (template) or top-level `title` (fallback)
- Support `tag:"plain_text"` elements
- Return title even when elements array is missing

**`extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts`**
- Add test: post-format fallback with nested arrays and title
- Add test: card template with `header.title.content`
- Add test: link extraction from `tag:"a"` elements with href

## Testing

- [x] Verified with real Feishu API responses from production bot
- [x] Existing card template format tests still pass (div/markdown)
- [x] 3 new test cases covering fallback scenarios

target.title: ui: default usage view to last 7 days instead of today-only
target.body:
## Summary

Fix the Control UI Usage tab default date range.

The Usage view currently initializes with:
- `usageStartDate = today`
- `usageEndDate = today`

That makes the default view show only a single day.

This change updates the initial state to:
- `usageStartDate = today - 6 days`
- `usageEndDate = today`

So the default Usage view covers the last 7 calendar days inclusive.

## Root cause

The issue is in `ui/src/ui/app.ts`, where the default Usage state is initialized.
`ui/src/ui/controllers/usage.ts` correctly uses whatever dates are already in state.

## Files changed

- `ui/src/ui/app.ts`

## Testing

Manual verification:
- Open Control UI
- Go to Usage
- Confirm default range is `today - 6 days` through `today`
