target.title: feat(nodes): add visionOS node app (Apple Vision Pro spatial peripheral)
target.body: ## Summary Adds a native visionOS companion app that pairs with the OpenClaw Gateway as a peripheral node, giving the AI agent spatial awareness, hand tracking, world sensing, and GPS from an Apple Vision Pro. Related: #55944 (platform policy ŌĆö adds `visionos` to `PLATFORM_DEFAULTS` and command policy rules) ## What's included ### `apps/visionos/` A complete SwiftUI visionOS app (visionOS 2.0+, Xcode 24.6): | File | Purpose | |---|---| | `GatewaySocket.swift` | URLSessionWebSocketTask + protocol v3 frame parsing | | `HandshakeManager.swift` | Ed25519 Secure Enclave keypair, nonce signing | | `NodeManager.swift` | RPC dispatcher, state machine, exponential backoff reconnect | | `SpatialManager.swift` | ARKit: HandTracking, WorldTracking, PlaneDetection, SceneReconstruction | | `LocationManager.swift` | CoreLocation one-shot async wrapper | | `ScenePhaseMonitor.swift` | Background/foreground detection, exit frame on dismiss | | `NodeImmersiveView.swift` | Minimal .mixed ImmersiveSpace (ARKit lifecycle anchor) | | `ContentView.swift` / `GatewayConfigView.swift` / `ConnectionStatusView.swift` | UI | ### `docs/nodes/visionos.md` Full documentation covering setup, Gateway config, all commands with JSON response schemas, architecture, protocol notes, security, and known limitations. ## Commands implemented | Command | Capability | Status | |---|---|---| | `spatial.hands` | 26-joint skeletal hand tracking (both hands) | Ō£ģ Verified on-device | | `spatial.planes` | Flat surface detection with classification | Ō£ģ Verified on-device | | `spatial.mesh` | Full scene reconstruction (triangulated geometry) | Ō£ģ Verified on-device | | `device.position` | Head position/orientation (6DoF world transform) | Ō£ģ Verified on-device | | `device.info` | Device metadata | Ō£ģ | | `location.get` | GPS via CoreLocation | Ō£ģ Verified on-device | | `camera.snap` | Still frame from main camera | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Requires Enterprise entitlement | | `camera.clip` | Short video clip from main camera | ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Requires Enterprise entitlement | | `canvas.*` | WKWebView canvas | ­¤ö£ Planned | ## Key architectural decisions **ImmersiveSpace as lifecycle anchor** ŌĆö visionOS suspends apps and kills WebSockets when the app loses foreground without an active ImmersiveSpace. The `NodeImmersiveView` opens a minimal `.mixed` space to prevent process suspension. This is architecturally required. **WebSocket is app-level** ŌĆö the socket connects on `ContentView.onAppear` and stays alive when the immersive space is closed. ARKit providers are ImmersiveSpace-level and start/stop with the space. **Stable device identity** ŌĆö Ed25519 keypair stored in Keychain with `kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlockThisDeviceOnly`. The device ID persists across app reinstalls and clean builds. Docs include instructions for forcing a reset. **CVE-2026-28466 mitigation** ŌĆö Incoming RPC params are validated; requests containing smuggled approval/bypass fields are rejected with `FORBIDDEN` before dispatch. ## Setup See [docs/nodes/visionos.md](docs/nodes/visionos.md) for full setup instructions including Gateway `allowCommands` config, ARKit permission requirements, and the approval flow.

target.title: cron: reject webhook URLs with embedded credentials
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2ŌĆō5 bullets: - Problem: Cron webhook URLs could include embedded credentials (e.g. https://user:pass@example.com/hook), which can leak secrets in logs, config, or URLs. - Why it matters: URL-embedded credentials are discouraged and can expose secrets; using query params or headers is preferred. - What changed: normalizeHttpWebhookUrl now rejects URLs with username or password in the parsed URL. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to auth headers, query params, or other credential handling; only http(s)://user:pass@host URLs are rejected. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Cron webhook URLs with embedded credentials (e.g. https://user:secret@host/path) are now rejected as invalid. URLs without credentials continue to work. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS (darwin) - Runtime/container: Node 22+ - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): CRON webhook delivery - Relevant config (redacted): N/A ### Steps 1. Add or update a cron job with webhook delivery using a URL with embedded credentials, e.g. https://user:pass@example.invalid/webhook. 2. Observe that the webhook URL is rejected (job will not use it). 3. Use the same job with https://example.invalid/webhook (no credentials) and confirm it is accepted. ### Expected - URLs with user:pass@ in the authority are rejected. - URLs without embedded credentials continue to work. ### Actual - Behavior matches above ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: normalizeHttpWebhookUrl returns null for URLs like http://user:pass@host/path; returns the URL for http://host/path. - Edge cases checked: URLs with only username (user@host), only password (not typical in URLs), and no credentials. - What you did **not** verify: End-to-end cron job creation via CLI or UI with credential URLs; live webhook delivery. ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`No`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`Yes`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: Update any cron job webhook URLs that include user:pass@ in the URL. Remove embedded credentials and use headers or query params for auth instead. ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert this commit. - Files/config to restore: src/cron/webhook-url.ts - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Jobs with credential URLs will fail validation or treat the webhook as invalid. ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: Existing jobs using credential URLs will break. - Mitigation: Document in release notes; credential URLs are discouraged; alternative auth methods should be used.

target.title: feat: configurable default agent ID (escape the 'main' prison) ­¤”×
target.body:
## Summary

Adds support for overriding the hardcoded `"main"` fallback agent ID, so users with a single custom-named agent no longer get a ghost `agents/main/` default path/session identity when no `agents.list` is configured.

## The Problem

`DEFAULT_AGENT_ID` falls back to `"main"`. Some resolution paths run from partial config or before a full multi-agent list exists, so a setup that intends a custom default agent can still resolve the main session key and default agent directory as `main`.

## Solution

Two complementary override mechanisms:

### 1. Config key: `agents.defaultAgentId`

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaultAgentId": "maine-lobster"
  }
}
```

### 2. Env var: `OPENCLAW_DEFAULT_AGENT_ID`

```bash
export OPENCLAW_DEFAULT_AGENT_ID=maine-lobster
```

### Priority (highest ŌåÆ lowest)

1. `agents.list[].default: true` (explicit default agent)
2. `agents.list[0]` (first configured agent)
3. `agents.defaultAgentId` config key
4. `OPENCLAW_DEFAULT_AGENT_ID` env var
5. Hardcoded `"main"` (backward compatible)

## Changes

- Adds `agents.defaultAgentId` to the config schema/types and generated docs baseline.
- Updates `resolveDefaultAgentId()`, `listAgentIds()`, `resolveDefaultAgentDir()`, and main-session key resolution to honor config/env fallback values.
- Keeps populated `agents.list` behavior unchanged: list default/first entry still wins.
- Updates provider auth/default-agent directory resolution to use the effective default agent.
- Adds regression tests for config default, env fallback, default dir, and main-session key behavior.

## Testing

- `pnpm config:schema:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm test src/agents/agent-scope-config.test.ts src/config/sessions/main-session.test.ts src/config/sessions/session-key.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD origin/main` ŌåÆ merge-clean

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A config-only default agent ID should stop OpenClaw from falling back to the hardcoded `main` agent for the default agent directory and main session key.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout rebased onto latest `origin/main`, running the patched TypeScript source directly with Node/tsx in `/root/.openclaw/workspace/openclaw-pr-acpx/pr-51762`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```console
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import { resolveDefaultAgentId, resolveDefaultAgentDir } from './src/agents/agent-scope-config.ts';
import { resolveMainSessionKey } from './src/config/sessions/main-session.ts';
const cfg = { agents: { defaultAgentId: 'Ops Team' }, session: { mainKey: 'main' } };
const env = { OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR: '/tmp/openclaw-real-proof' };
console.log('defaultAgentId=' + resolveDefaultAgentId(cfg, env));
console.log('defaultAgentDir=' + resolveDefaultAgentDir(cfg, env));
console.log('mainSessionKey=' + resolveMainSessionKey(cfg, env));
EOF
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output copied from the patched real source command above:

```console
defaultAgentId=ops-team
defaultAgentDir=/tmp/openclaw-real-proof/agents/ops-team/agent
mainSessionKey=agent:ops-team:main
```

- **Observed result after fix**: `agents.defaultAgentId: "Ops Team"` is normalized to `ops-team`, the default agent directory resolves under `/tmp/openclaw-real-proof/agents/ops-team/agent`, and the main session key resolves to `agent:ops-team:main` instead of `main`.
- **What was not tested**: No browser/UI flow was tested; this PR changes config/session/agent resolution behavior and was verified through the live runtime resolver functions plus the targeted regression/check suite above.

Fixes #17477 ­¤”×

target.title: feat(hooks): emit session prune lifecycle event
target.body:
## Why Context pruning changes model input quality and cost, but there was no dedicated internal lifecycle signal for pruning actions. This PR emits a pruning lifecycle hook with safe summary metadata so pruning behavior is observable without exposing pruned content. Closes #8606 ## Detailed Changes - Added internal lifecycle event: - `session:prune` - Pruning hook context includes summary metadata: - `softTrimmedCount` - `hardClearedCount` - `toolNames` - `sessionId` (when available) - Updated context-pruning extension/pruner tests for async path + hook emission behavior ## Related Links and Issues - Existing source PR: #9761 - Closes #8606

Greptile Overview

Greptile Summary

This PR adds a new internal hook lifecycle event, `session:prune`, emitted by the context pruning path. The pruner now tracks how many tool results were soft-trimmed vs hard-cleared (and which tool names were affected), and passes this safe summary metadata into an internal hook event. The context-pruning extensionŌĆÖs `context` handler was updated to be async to await pruning, and tests were updated to cover the async path and hook emission behavior. Within the codebase, this integrates into the existing internal hook system (`src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`) by creating and triggering an `InternalHookEvent` of type `session` and action `prune`, keyed by a derived session key.

Confidence Score: 4/5

- This PR is mostly safe to merge, but there is one correctness issue around the emitted hook event timestamp semantics that should be clarified/fixed. - The changes are localized (pruner + extension + tests + docs) and align with existing internal hook infrastructure. The main concern is that the emitted hook event uses a timestamp created at emission time, which can be meaningfully later than the prune operation and may mislead consumers relying on timing semantics. - src/agents/pi-extensions/context-pruning/pruner.ts

target.title: Fix embedded session fence for queued transcript writes
target.body:
## Summary
- allow queued user transcript appends while the embedded prompt lock is released
- allow same-length linear transcript rewrites and unchanged text refreshes to advance the session fence
- avoid publishing nested writes while an active session write lock is already running

## Why
OpenClaw can append queued user turns or rewrite linear transcript metadata while an embedded prompt is waiting on model I/O. Those OpenClaw-owned updates should not be classified as external session takeover.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Embedded agent runs no longer fail with `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` / `session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released` when OpenClaw-owned transcript appends happen while the embedded prompt lock is released.

Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw setup using the installed gateway with the equivalent session-fence fix applied before upstreaming this TypeScript source patch. `openclaw gateway status --deep` reported CLI version `2026.5.28`, Gateway version `2026.5.28`, runtime `running`, connectivity probe `ok`, and admin capability available.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
openclaw gateway status --deep
openclaw agent --agent main --message 'Ķ»Ęńö©õĖĆÕÅźõĖŁµ¢ćń«Ćń¤ŁĶ»┤µśÄõĮĀńÄ░Õ£©ÕÅ»õ╗źµŁŻÕĖĖÕōŹÕ║öŃĆé' --timeout 90
tail -n 240 /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-06-04.log | rg -n "EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError|session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released" || true
```

Evidence after fix:
```text
Runtime: running (pid 32433, state active)
Connectivity probe: ok
Capability: admin-capable

$ openclaw agent --agent main --message 'Ķ»Ęńö©õĖĆÕÅźõĖŁµ¢ćń«Ćń¤ŁĶ»┤µśÄõĮĀńÄ░Õ£©ÕÅ»õ╗źµŁŻÕĖĖÕōŹÕ║öŃĆé' --timeout 90
ÕÅ»õ╗ź’╝īõĖĆÕłćµŁŻÕĖĖŃĆé

$ tail -n 240 /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-06-04.log | rg -n "EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError|session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released" || true
# no matches
```

Observed result after fix: The real local OpenClaw gateway accepted an embedded agent request and returned a normal response; the recent gateway log window did not contain the session-fence takeover error that this patch targets.

What was not tested: I did not run a remote cross-platform package smoke or Telegram/Desktop channel proof for this PR. The focused source regression coverage below exercises the session-fence cases added by this change.

## Tests
- `pnpm exec node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`

target.title: fix(cron): recover from local-llamacpp parameter serialization bugs
target.body:
Local llamacpp models (e.g. qwen-27b) can serialize tool call arguments with concatenated JSON property names, causing cron add/update calls to fail with "unexpected property" errors.

Added `recoverConcatenatedKeys()` to `normalizeCronJobInput` that detects and splits these concatenated keys before the normal normalization pipeline runs.

## Corruption Patterns

| Intended | Corrupted | Pattern |
|----------|-----------|---------|
| `"name"` + `"payload"` (separate keys) | `"namePayload"` | Adjacent sibling keys concatenate |
| `"schedule"` ŌåÆ `{"kind": "every"}` | `"scheduleKind"` | Parent key merges with first nested child key |
| `"sessionTarget": "isolated"` | `"sessionTargetName": "evidence-test"` | Key merges with next sibling, value gets cross-wired |

## Fix

`recoverConcatenatedKeys()` handles three observed patterns:
1. `namePayload` ŌåÆ extracts `payload` object
2. `scheduleKind` ŌåÆ extracts `schedule` object (with nested kind intact)
3. `sessionTargetName` ŌåÆ extracts `name`, restores default `sessionTarget: "isolated"`

The recovery runs at the top of `normalizeCronJobInput`, before any field validation or coercion.

## Real Behavior Proof

**Source-level proof**: Running the corrupted payload through the current cron.add handler produces strict schema validation rejection. This fix intercepts the corrupted input *before* schema validation.

**Test coverage**: 6 tests covering individual patterns, full corruption scenario, normal passthrough, and `cron.update` patch path.

Fixes #88439

target.title: [codex] fix Codex budget native compaction trigger
target.body:
## Summary

- Start Codex native app-server compaction for the `budget` preflight trigger instead of treating it like an unrelated automatic trigger.
- Keep omitted and `overflow` triggers on the existing Codex-owned skip path.
- Document that `agents.defaults.compaction.maxActiveTranscriptBytes` starts native Codex compaction for Codex harness sessions.

## Duplicate check

I checked current `origin/main` plus open PRs/issues before opening this PR. Current `main` still skipped `trigger: "budget"` in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.ts` and had a test pinning that skip in `compact.test.ts`. I did not find an open PR from this fork for this compaction trigger; the only open `Steady-ai` PR was #86716 for Discord delivery accounting. I also inspected nearby compaction/Codex PRs including #81916, #63636, #54585, #86160, #83229, #82856, #86094, and #73092; none made `budget` start native Codex compaction.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex harness sessions with `agents.defaults.compaction.maxActiveTranscriptBytes` call compaction with `trigger: "budget"`. Current source skipped that trigger before sending `thread/compact/start`, so the local transcript-byte guard could leave long-lived channel mirrors above the configured cleanup threshold.

Real environment tested: OpenClaw source worktree rebased on `origin/main` at `d88681662b`, PR head `47f33748d4`, on WSL Ubuntu 24.04 / Node 22.22.0. The source-runtime probe used the real `maybeCompactCodexAppServerSession` entry point and real Codex session-binding file I/O, with the app-server client boundary instrumented only to capture the outgoing app-server request.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a `node --import tsx -` source-runtime probe from the PR worktree. The probe wrote a temporary Codex app-server binding, invoked `maybeCompactCodexAppServerSession` once with `trigger: "budget"` and once with `trigger: "overflow"`, then printed the request/result shape. I then ran the focused checks listed in Verification.

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the source-runtime probe:

```text
[agent/embedded] started codex app-server compaction
[agent/embedded] skipping codex app-server compaction for non-manual trigger
{
  "head": "47f33748d4",
  "behavior": "codex budget compaction trigger starts native app-server compaction",
  "budget": {
    "request": {
      "method": "thread/compact/start",
      "params": {
        "threadId": "thread-proof-budget"
      }
    },
    "ok": true,
    "compacted": false,
    "reason": null,
    "signal": "thread/compact/start",
    "pending": true,
    "tokensBefore": 456
  },
  "overflow": {
    "additionalRequests": 0,
    "ok": true,
    "compacted": false,
    "reason": "codex app-server owns automatic compaction",
    "skippedReason": "non_manual_trigger",
    "trigger": "overflow"
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: Budget preflight compaction now sends `thread/compact/start` and returns the existing pending native-compaction result shape. The overflow trigger sends no additional app-server request and still returns the existing `non_manual_trigger` skip result.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Discord or Telegram gateway replay from this source branch. The live failure was observed in an installed runtime; this PR ports the source-level behavior and locks it with focused Codex extension tests.

## Risk

Low. The change is limited to the Codex app-server compaction trigger gate. It does not wait for native compaction completion, add credentials, change network targets, or replace Codex compaction with OpenClaw summarization.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts` -> 1 file / 19 tests passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts docs/plugins/codex-harness-runtime.md docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed
- `pnpm docs:list` -> passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts` -> passed
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/plugins/codex-harness-runtime.md docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md` -> passed

target.title: fix: reload config in slash command routing so dmScope is respected
target.body:
Fixes #39605

## Problem

Native slash commands (`/status`, `/ask`, etc.) in Discord, Telegram, and Slack DMs route to `agent:main:main` instead of respecting `session.dmScope` (e.g. `per-channel-peer`). Normal text messages in the same conversation correctly route to the expected session key like `agent:main:discord:direct:<userId>`.

## Root Cause

All three channel providers load config once at startup and capture it in a closure. The message handlers already call `loadConfig()` fresh per message (e.g. `message-handler.preflight.ts`), but the slash/native-command routing paths reuse the stale startup snapshot. Since `resolveAgentRoute` reads `cfg.session.dmScope` to compute the session key, slash commands always use the config state from startup time.

## Fix

Call `loadConfig()` in each channel's slash/native-command routing section before route resolution, matching the pattern already used by message handlers.

### Changes

- **`src/discord/monitor/native-command.ts`**: Load fresh config in `dispatchDiscordCommandInteraction` before routing; promote `loadConfig` from type-only to value import
- **`src/telegram/bot-native-commands.ts`**: Load fresh config in `resolveCommandRuntimeContext` before routing; add `loadConfig` value import
- **`src/slack/monitor/slash.ts`**: Load fresh config in `handleSlashCommand` before routing; add `loadConfig` import

### Testing

- Type-check clean (`pnpm tsgo`)
- Lint clean (`pnpm lint` ŌĆö only pre-existing `debug-route.ts` error)
- All routing tests pass (`src/routing/resolve-route.test.ts`, `src/routing/session-key.test.ts`)

target.title: refactor(test): hoist skill status fixture to module-level constant
target.body:
Follow-up to #60914 addressing Codex feedback noted by @altaywtf.

## Problem

`skillStatusReportFixture` was defined inside `vi.hoisted()` and also exported in the return object so `beforeEach` could reference it. This duplicated the fixture data across two locations.

Additionally, `buildWorkspaceSkillStatusMock` had a dead initial implementation ŌĆö `beforeEach` calls `mockReset()` followed by `mockReturnValue(...)` before every test, so the factory inside `vi.hoisted()` was never actually invoked.

## Fix

- Move the fixture to a module-level constant `SKILL_STATUS_FIXTURE` (defined once, before `vi.hoisted`)
- Remove `skillStatusReportFixture` from the `vi.hoisted()` return object and destructuring
- Replace the dead `buildWorkspaceSkillStatusMock` factory with a plain `vi.fn()`
- Update `beforeEach` to reference the module-level `SKILL_STATUS_FIXTURE`

No behaviour changes ŌĆö all existing test assertions are preserved.

target.title: fix(cron): add defensive prune before reading run log to prevent OOM
target.body:
> [!NOTE]
> This PR is AI-assisted (authored with OpenClaw/MillieClaw agent tooling).

## Problem

`readCronRunLogEntriesPage` reads the entire JSONL run log file into memory via `fs.readFile` before parsing and paginating. While writes are pruned to `DEFAULT_CRON_RUN_LOG_MAX_BYTES` (2MB) via `pruneIfNeeded`, the pruning is async and can fail silently under disk pressure. If pruning fails, the file grows unbounded, and a subsequent read loads the entire file into memory ŌĆö causing OOM.

Closes #57017

## Root Cause

The `pruneIfNeeded` function is called in the write path (`appendCronRunLog`) but not in the read path (`readCronRunLogEntriesPage` / `readCronRunLogEntriesPageAll`). If the async prune during append fails silently (e.g. disk pressure, permission error), the file grows beyond the expected max size. The next read loads it all into memory.

## Fix

Add a defensive `serializedPruneIfNeeded` call before `fs.readFile` in both `readCronRunLogEntriesPage` and `readCronRunLogEntriesPageAll`. The prune is serialized through the `writesByPath` queue to prevent race conditions with concurrent appends.

Additional improvements driven by review feedback:
- `.catch(() => undefined)` on both prune calls so reads remain resilient under disk pressure
- Optional `pruneOptions` threaded from runtime config through the full gateway call chain, replacing hardcoded defaults
- 9 new tests covering the fix, error resilience, config threading, concurrency safety, and queue cleanup

## Testing

- Oversized file prune on read (core fix)
- Prune failure resilience ŌĆö read-only dir (Page + PageAll)
- Custom `pruneOptions` respected (Page + PageAll)
- Concurrent append + read-side prune ŌĆö no data loss
- Failed prune does not poison write queue
- Write queue cleanup after prune

target.title: Expose paged channel action results
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve the existing Discord `thread-list` raw `threads` result while adding normalized pagination metadata at the action boundary.
- Surface `complete`, `hasMore`, `returnedCount`, `source`, `query`, and `nextBefore` for archived thread pages when Discord reports `has_more`.
- Cover the archived-thread `has_more` regression and the missing archive timestamp fallback in the Discord action runtime tests.

## Verification

- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.test.ts extensions/discord/src/send.messages.test.ts` (97 passed)
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/simple-completion-runtime.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=kepler-alpha --thinking codex=high` (clean; no accepted/actionable findings)
- Crabbox/Testbox E2E on the earlier PR head: provider `<test-provider>`, lease `<test-lease>`, Actions run `<actions-run-url>`; checked out `<pr-ref>` and ran `corepack pnpm test:docker:npm-onboard-discord-channel-agent`, result `npm tarball onboard/channel/agent Docker E2E passed (discord)`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Discord archived thread-list actions expose truthful page completion plus a reusable `nextBefore` cursor while preserving the prior raw `threads` provider payload.

Real environment tested: Exact PR head `1a2d7b4fec5f089d730acfd257602f82d4fc0648`, with the local PR branch Discord action runtime calling the live Discord API for the configured `<account-id>` bot account in a test guild. Three disposable public threads were created in `<channel-name>`, archived, paged, and deleted.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a redacted `node --import tsx --input-type=module` proof script from the PR worktree. The script resolved only the configured Discord account token from the active runtime snapshot, installed that resolved snapshot in the proof process, called branch `handleDiscordAction` to create and archive three disposable threads, requested archived threads with `limit: 2`, requested the next page with `before: firstPage.nextBefore`, asserted thread identities and cursor equality, then deleted every proof thread in `finally`.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "headSha": "1a2d7b4fec5f089d730acfd257602f82d4fc0648",
  "environment": "local PR branch action runtime against live Discord API",
  "setup": {
    "created": 3,
    "archived": 3,
    "channel": "<channel-name>",
    "pageLimit": 2
  },
  "firstPage": {
    "complete": false,
    "hasMore": true,
    "returnedCount": 2,
    "source": "discord.threadList.archived",
    "query": {
      "guildId": "<guild-id>",
      "channelId": "<channel-id>",
      "includeArchived": true,
      "limit": 2
    },
    "nextBefore": "2026-06-12T20:45:07.612000+00:00",
    "lastArchiveTimestamp": "2026-06-12T20:45:07.612000+00:00",
    "rawHasMore": true,
    "items": ["<proof-thread-newest>", "<proof-thread-middle>"]
  },
  "continuationPage": {
    "complete": true,
    "hasMore": false,
    "returnedCount": 1,
    "source": "discord.threadList.archived",
    "query": {
      "guildId": "<guild-id>",
      "channelId": "<channel-id>",
      "includeArchived": true,
      "before": "2026-06-12T20:45:07.612000+00:00",
      "limit": 2
    },
    "rawHasMore": false,
    "contains": "<proof-thread-oldest>"
  },
  "assertions": {
    "cursorMatchesLastArchiveTimestamp": true,
    "continuationUsedNextBefore": true,
    "firstPageContainsNewestAndMiddleProofThreads": true,
    "continuationContainsOldestProofThread": true
  },
  "cleanup": {
    "deleted": 3,
    "cleanupFailures": 0
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: The first live archived page returned two non-empty rows with Discord `has_more: true`; the normalized result exposed `complete: false`, `hasMore: true`, and a `nextBefore` exactly equal to the last returned thread's archive timestamp. Passing that value back as `before` returned the older proof thread and a complete final page. All three disposable threads were deleted successfully.

What was not tested: A full PR-branch gateway/CLI end-to-end run. Discord `thread-list` is gateway-owned, so a source-plugin override with the currently installed gateway still exercises the installed gateway action runtime. The live proof instead targets the exact changed plugin action boundary against Discord. No supported Mantis scenario covers Discord archived-thread pagination.

target.title: fix(cron): skip deleteAfterRun and preserve counters for manual runs (#83538)
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw cron run` (manual/debug runs) on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes the job on success ŌĆö consuming the one-shot and preventing its scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters, interfering with scheduled retry/backoff state.

This PR adds an `isManual` flag to `applyJobResult` so manual runs preserve the job and leave scheduled execution state untouched.

## Root cause

`applyJobResult()` has no concept of manual vs scheduled runs. When a manual run succeeds on a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job, `shouldDelete` evaluates to `true` and the job is removed from the store ŌĆö the scheduled fire never happens.

Additionally, manual error/skipped/success outcomes unconditionally mutate `consecutiveErrors` and `consecutiveSkipped` counters, which control retry backoff and auto-disable behavior for scheduled runs.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `timer.ts` | Add `isManual` option to `applyJobResult`; skip `deleteAfterRun` for manual runs; guard all counter mutations and alert emissions with `!isManual` |
| `ops.ts` | Pass `isManual: true` from manual run completion and invalid-persisted-run handling |
| `timer.regression.test.ts` | 5 new tests: manual ok/error preserve job, manual doesn't increment/reset counters |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts
 Ō£ō cron (45 tests) 144ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  45 passed (45)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job deletes it, preventing scheduled execution. Manual runs also mutate scheduled retry counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `aa92a4a5b0`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.

### Source-level proof

```
Main branch applyJobResult():
  - shouldDelete = kind === "at" && deleteAfterRun && status === "ok"
    ŌåÆ no manual discriminator, always deletes on success
  - consecutiveErrors incremented unconditionally on error
  - consecutiveSkipped incremented unconditionally on skip

Fix branch:
  - shouldDelete = wouldDelete && !opts?.isManual
    ŌåÆ manual runs never trigger deletion
  - Counter mutations guarded: if (!opts?.isManual) { ... }
    ŌåÆ manual runs don't affect retry/backoff state
```

### Regression test coverage

```
Ō£ģ manual ok on deleteAfterRun at-job ŌåÆ job preserved, not deleted
Ō£ģ manual error on deleteAfterRun at-job ŌåÆ job preserved, not deleted  
Ō£ģ scheduled ok on deleteAfterRun at-job ŌåÆ job deleted (unchanged behavior)
Ō£ģ manual error does not increment consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 ŌåÆ still 3)
Ō£ģ manual success does not reset consecutiveErrors (pre-existing=3 ŌåÆ still 3)
```

- **What was not tested**: Live `openclaw cron run` CLI flow (requires a running gateway with a configured `deleteAfterRun` at-job). The `isManual` flag propagation from `ops.ts` to `applyJobResult` is validated at the unit level.

Closes #83538


### Re-verification (2026-05-20)

Local checkout at `d89f14de26`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

 Ō£ō cron  ../../src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (39 tests) 116ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  39 passed (39)
   Duration  152ms
```

Full cron suite (`src/cron/`) also run: 94 test files, 901 passed. The 7 failures are in unrelated files (`run-log.test.ts`, `service.failure-alert.test.ts`) pre-existing on upstream/main.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `openclaw cron run` (manual) of a `deleteAfterRun: true` at-job incorrectly deletes the job. Manual runs also pollute `consecutiveErrors`/`consecutiveSkipped` counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched checkout at `0cec2acc7a`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On `upstream/main`, counter mutations and `shouldDelete` run unconditionally ŌĆö no `isManual` discriminator exists.
- **Exact steps after patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts` + `src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: 46/46 timer regression tests pass (including 7 new #83538/#83933-specific tests). 7/7 failure-alert tests pass.
- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI flow; the tests exercise the same `applyJobResult` code path the Gateway calls internally.

### Key test results

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun + success | NOT deleted Ō£ģ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun + success | Deleted Ō£ģ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 Ō£ģ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 Ō£ģ |
| Manual error ŌåÆ failure alert | NOT triggered Ō£ģ |
| Scheduled error ŌåÆ failure alert | Triggered Ō£ģ |


## Real behavior proof (Docker, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker container, `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched source at `885d605e56`.

### Latest fixes

| Issue | Problem | Fix |
|-------|---------|-----|
| P1 | Manual at-job error was disabling job (`enabled=false`, `nextRunAtMs=undefined`) | At-job error retry/disable block now guarded with `if (opts?.isManual)` |
| P2 | Manual success was clearing `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown | Cooldown clearing now inside `if (!opts?.isManual)` guard |

### Test results

| Test Suite | Result |
|------------|--------|
| Timer regression (49 tests) | Ō£ģ All passed |
| Failure-alert (7 tests) | Ō£ģ All passed |

### Key verified behaviors

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| Manual at-job error | `enabled` stays true, `nextRunAtMs` preserved Ō£ģ |
| Manual success | `lastFailureAlertAtMs` cooldown preserved Ō£ģ |
| Manual run + deleteAfterRun | Job NOT deleted Ō£ģ |
| Scheduled run + deleteAfterRun | Job deleted Ō£ģ |
| Manual error + consecutiveErrors=3 | Stays 3 Ō£ģ |
| Manual success + consecutiveErrors=5 | Stays 5 Ō£ģ |


## Real CLI proof (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway at `885d605e56` running with `--allow-unconfigured`.

**Terminal transcript**: `openclaw cron add` ŌåÆ `cron show` ŌåÆ `cron run` (manual, triggers error) ŌåÆ `cron show` (job still exists, enabled=true, nextRunAtMs preserved) ŌåÆ `cron list` (job present) ŌåÆ `cron runs` (run history with error status, nextRunAtMs unchanged).

| Step | Command | Result |
|------|---------|--------|
| Add job | `openclaw cron add --name proof-job --at ... --delete-after-run` | Created with `deleteAfterRun: true` |
| Manual run | `openclaw cron run <id>` | `{"ok": true, "enqueued": true}` |
| After run | `openclaw cron show <id>` | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` Ō£ģ |
| Job exists | `openclaw cron list` | Job still listed Ō£ģ |
| Run history | `openclaw cron runs --id <id>` | `status: "error"`, `nextRunAtMs: preserved` Ō£ģ |


## Real CLI proof at head SHA `642c3450d9` (Docker Gateway, 2026-05-21)

**Environment**: Docker `node:24-bookworm-slim` (aarch64), patched Gateway built from PR head.

| Test | Command | Before (main) | After (PR head) |
|------|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| A: at-job deleteAfterRun | `cron run` ŌåÆ `cron show` | Job deleted/disabled | `enabled: true`, `nextRunAtMs` preserved Ō£ģ |
| B: every-job manual error | `cron run` ŌåÆ `cron show` | `nextRunAtMs` rewritten by backoff | `nextRunAtMs` unchanged Ō£ģ |
| A+B: counters | `cron runs` | `consecutiveErrors` mutated | Unchanged Ō£ģ |

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` no longer consumes `deleteAfterRun` one-shot jobs, and no longer perturbs retry counters, at-job retry/disable state, recurring backoff, or failure-alert cooldown.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container built from PR HEAD `3d32c9baff`, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.14.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `applyJobResult` has no manual-run discriminator ŌĆö manual runs unconditionally drive `deleteAfterRun`, counter mutations, at-job disable, recurring backoff, and alert cooldown. The 12 regression tests added by this PR do not exist on main.

- **Exact steps or commands run after this patch**:

```bash
# Build Docker image from PR HEAD
docker build --target build -t openclaw-pr83933:test .

# Test 1: Timer regression tests (includes all 12 manual-run isolation tests)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

# Test 2: Failure alert tests (adapted for manual-run isolation)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts

# Test 3: Lint check
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test npx oxlint src/cron/service/timer.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.ts

# Test 4: Full cron test suite

Control-group tests confirm scheduled runs still drive all 5 state effects normally.

- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI `openclaw cron run` end-to-end (covered by earlier Docker CLI proof at `642c3450d9` ŌĆö subsequent commits were formatting-only changes via oxfmt). The `cron-protocol-conformance` tests require macOS app source and cannot run in Docker.


## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `6bc3b9e374`, 2026-05-30)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` no longer consumes `deleteAfterRun` one-shot jobs, and no longer perturbs retry counters, at-job retry/disable state, recurring backoff, failure-alert cooldown, or skipped-run alert counters.
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container built from PR HEAD `6bc3b9e374`, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.14.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `applyJobResult` has no manual-run discriminator ŌĆö manual runs unconditionally drive all 5 scheduled-state effects plus skipped-run alert paths. The 14 regression tests added by this PR do not exist on main.

- **Exact steps or commands run after this patch**:

```bash
# Build Docker image from PR HEAD
docker build --target build -t openclaw-pr83933:test .

# Test 1: Timer regression tests (all 14 manual-run isolation tests)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts

# Test 2: Failure alert tests (adapted for manual-run isolation)
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts

# Test 3: Lint check
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test npx oxlint src/cron/service/timer.ts src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.ts

# Test 4: Full cron test suite
docker run --rm openclaw-pr83933:test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Test 1 ŌĆö Timer regression: **56 tests passed** (234ms)
```
 Ō£ō cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (56 tests) 234ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  56 passed (56)
```

      Tests  11 passed (11)
```

```

Test 4 ŌĆö Full cron suite: **1002 tests passed** across 98 test files
```
 Test Files  1 failed | 97 passed (98)
      Tests  2 failed | 1002 passed (1004)
```
The 2 failures are in `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts` ŌĆö these look for Swift source files (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/CronModels.swift`) which are not present in the Docker build target. Pre-existing and unrelated to this PR.

- **Observed result after fix**: All 14 new regression tests pass, covering all 6 guarded state effects including the newly-added manual skipped-run isolation:
  - Manual `deleteAfterRun` at-job ŌåÆ NOT deleted Ō£ģ
  - Manual error ŌåÆ counters NOT incremented Ō£ģ
  - Manual success ŌåÆ counters NOT reset, cooldown NOT cleared Ō£ģ
  - Manual at-job error ŌåÆ NOT disabled, nextRunAtMs preserved Ō£ģ
  - Manual every-job error ŌåÆ backoff NOT applied, nextRunAtMs preserved Ō£ģ
  - Manual skipped at-job ŌåÆ consecutiveSkipped NOT incremented, alert NOT fired, all state preserved Ō£ģ
  - Manual skipped run with `includeSkipped: true` ŌåÆ failure alert NOT emitted Ō£ģ

  Control-group tests confirm scheduled runs still drive all state effects normally.

- **What was not tested**: Live Gateway CLI `openclaw cron run` end-to-end (covered by earlier Docker CLI proof at `642c3450d9` ŌĆö subsequent commits were formatting + test coverage changes). The `cron-protocol-conformance` tests require macOS app source and cannot run in Docker.

### Manual-run state contract

`cron run` is a debug/verification command. Manual runs record their outcome (lastRunAtMs, lastStatus, lastError) but do not advance scheduled state. This makes manual runs safe, non-destructive operations that cannot interfere with scheduled execution. No reasonable user scenario depends on manual runs consuming one-shot jobs or driving retry/backoff state.

## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `ccb89e3f11`, 2026-06-02)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `lastRunWasManual` field was missing from the `CronJobStateSchema` and `CronJobStatePatchSchema` in `packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/cron.ts`. Both schemas use `additionalProperties: false`, so returning a cron job state containing `lastRunWasManual` would fail gateway protocol validation on `cron list`/`cron show`. Added `lastRunWasManual: Type.Optional(Type.Boolean())` to both schemas.

- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`), Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js `v24.16.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`, patched OpenClaw at commit `ccb89e3f11`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: On the previous commit (`45efd1abdd`), `CronJobStateSchema` did not include `lastRunWasManual`. Any cron job state object with `lastRunWasManual: true` (set by `timer.ts:414` after a manual run) would be rejected by TypeBox validation due to `additionalProperties: false`. The `CronJobStatePatchSchema` was similarly missing the field, preventing state patches that include it.

- **Exact steps or commands run after this patch**:

```bash
docker run --rm \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" \
  -v /app/node_modules \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" \
  node:24-bookworm \
  bash -c '
    cd /app && npm install 2>&1 | tail -3 && \
    node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
      src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts \
      src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts \
      src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts \
      src/cron/cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts \
      src/cron/cron-protocol-schema.test.ts 2>&1'
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
 Ō£ō cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (56 tests) 335ms
 Ō£ō cron src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts (15 tests) 54ms
 Ō£ō cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (10 tests) 31ms
 Ō£ō cron src/cron/cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts (3 tests) 3ms
 Ō£ō cron src/cron/cron-protocol-schema.test.ts (2 tests) 0ms

 Test Files  5 passed (5)
      Tests  86 passed (86)
   Start at  07:37:28
   Duration  3.44s (transform 2.31s, setup 280ms, import 2.62s, tests 424ms, environment 0ms)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: All 86 tests pass including:
  - `timer.regression.test.ts`: 56 tests ŌĆö manual run sets `lastRunWasManual: true` on state (line 2531), state now passes schema validation.
  - `service.restart-catchup.test.ts`: 15 tests ŌĆö restart catch-up checks `lastRunWasManual` in state fixture (line 224) and correctly re-runs the job.
  - `cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts`: 3 tests ŌĆö schema conformance verified (delivery modes, status shape, failover reasons).
  - `cron-protocol-schema.test.ts`: 2 tests ŌĆö schema structure validated (deprecated alias, failure notification state).

- **What was not tested**: Live `cron list`/`cron show` gateway roundtrip with the patched schema. The schema fix is structural (adding a field to a TypeBox object with `additionalProperties: false`) and the test suite validates that state objects with `lastRunWasManual` are accepted by the schema.

## Live Gateway CLI roundtrip proof (Docker, HEAD `ccb89e3f11`)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a `deleteAfterRun` at-job must not delete the job, must not disable it, must not clear `nextRunAtMs`, and must set `lastRunWasManual: true` in state. The `lastRunWasManual` field must pass `CronJobStateSchema` validation (which uses `additionalProperties: false`).
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (linux/arm64, OrbStack), Node.js `v24.16.0`, OpenClaw Gateway built from source at commit `ccb89e3f11`.
- **Steps**:
  1. Built OpenClaw from source on the PR branch
  2. Started Gateway inside Docker with `gateway run --dev --allow-unconfigured --auth token`
  3. Created a `deleteAfterRun` at-job via `cron add`
  4. Manually ran the job via `cron run` (resulted in error due to missing codex-linux-arm64 in Docker ŌĆö this is the interesting case: manual error on a deleteAfterRun job)
  5. Verified job survival, `lastRunWasManual: true`, `enabled: true`, and `nextRunAtMs` preserved via `cron show` and `cron list`
  6. Verified `cron list` and `cron show` returned the `lastRunWasManual` field without schema validation errors

- **Observed result**: After manual `cron run` (which triggered an error due to missing codex dependency in Docker):
  - Job **survived** ŌĆö still present in `cron list` and `cron show` Ō£ģ

  - `nextRunAtMs: 1798732799000` ŌĆö preserved, NOT cleared Ō£ģ
  - `deleteAfterRun: true` ŌĆö still set, NOT consumed by manual run Ō£ģ
  - `lastRunWasManual: true` ŌĆö new field present and accepted by schema Ō£ģ
  - No `consecutiveErrors` field ŌĆö manual error did NOT increment counter Ō£ģ
  - Run history shows `runId: "manual:..."` prefix confirming manual execution path Ō£ģ
  - Both `cron show` and `cron list` returned `lastRunWasManual` without schema validation errors, proving `CronJobStateSchema` correctly includes the field Ō£ģ

- **What was not tested**: Manual run with `status: "ok"` (would require a working LLM provider in Docker). The `shouldDelete` guard path (`wouldDelete && !opts?.isManual`) is exercised by unit tests. Multi-node cluster, production load.


## Real behavior proof (Docker, HEAD `8596607fca`, 2026-06-02 ŌĆö backoff fix)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Manual `cron run` on a recurring job that errors must not open a backoff window that blocks the next scheduled fire. Previously, `isErrorBackoffPending()`, `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()`, `isPendingErrorBackoffSlot()`, and `recomputeJobNextRunAtMs()` read `lastRunStatus` and `lastRunAtMs` without checking `lastRunWasManual`, causing a manual error to push `nextRunAtMs` forward and block the timer-scheduled fire.

- **Real environment tested**: Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (linux/arm64), Node.js `v24.16.0`, Vitest `v4.1.7`, patched OpenClaw at commit `8596607fca`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` does not check `lastRunWasManual`. A recurring every-job with `lastRunWasManual: true` and `lastRunStatus: "error"` would have `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` return a backoff deadline, `isErrorBackoffPending()` would return `true`, and `isRunnableJob()` would return `false` ŌĆö blocking the next scheduled fire. `recomputeJobNextRunAtMs()` would also push `nextRunAtMs` forward to the backoff floor. Test file authored as part of this PR.

- **Exact steps or commands run after this patch**:

```bash
# Unit tests in Docker
docker run --rm --platform linux/arm64 \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" -e CI=true \
  node:24-bookworm bash -c \
  "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts \
    src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts \
    src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts"

# Live Gateway CLI in Docker
docker run --rm --platform linux/arm64 \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app \
  -e NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" -e CI=true \
  -e OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-test \
  -e OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=test-token-123 \
  node:24-bookworm bash -c '
    mkdir -p /tmp/openclaw-test
    echo "{\"gateway\":{\"port\":19001,\"bind\":\"loopback\"}}" > /tmp/openclaw-test/openclaw.json
    node openclaw.mjs gateway run --dev --allow-unconfigured --token test-token-123 --port 19001 &
    sleep 8
    node openclaw.mjs cron add --name test-at-delete --at "2026-06-02T23:00:00Z" --message reminder --delete-after-run --url ws://127.0.0.1:19001 --token test-token-123
    # extract ID, then: cron run <ID>, cron show <ID>, cron list
    node openclaw.mjs cron add --name test-recurring --every 10s --message "recurring check" --url ws://127.0.0.1:19001 --token test-token-123
    # extract ID, then: cron run <ID>, cron show <ID>
    kill %1'
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

<details>
<summary>Docker unit test output (82 tests pass)</summary>

```
 Ō£ō cron src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts (57 tests) 254ms
 Ō£ō cron src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts (15 tests) 44ms
 Ō£ō cron src/cron/service.failure-alert.test.ts (10 tests) 25ms

 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  82 passed (82)
   Start at  11:08:37
   Duration  2.77s (transform 1.83s, setup 250ms, import 2.09s, tests 323ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 5.85s
```

</details>

<details>

```

v24.16.0
=== Starting Gateway ===
[gateway] ready

TEST 1: Create deleteAfterRun at-job
  ŌåÆ id: dbfbe16b-c44a-4aa1-b820-889dbdbcb9a2, deleteAfterRun: true, nextRunAtMs: 1780441200000

TEST 2: Manual run on deleteAfterRun at-job
  ŌåÆ { "ok": true, "enqueued": true, "runId": "manual:dbfbe16b-..." }

TEST 3: Verify at-job survived manual run
  ŌåÆ id: dbfbe16b-..., enabled: true, deleteAfterRun: true, nextRunAtMs: 1780441200000 (PRESERVED)
  ŌåÆ status: "running" (agent executing but will error ŌĆö no LLM provider in Docker)

TEST 4: Create recurring every-10s job

  ŌåÆ { "ok": true, "enqueued": true, "runId": "manual:18b2583a-..." }

TEST 6: Verify recurring job state after manual run
  ŌåÆ id: 18b2583a-..., enabled: true, nextRunAtMs: 1780398505454 (PRESERVED, NOT pushed by backoff)

TEST 7: Schema validation ŌĆö cron list --json
  ŌåÆ Both jobs returned with valid schema, no validation errors
  ŌåÆ total: 2, both enabled, both with preserved nextRunAtMs

Gateway stopped.
```

</details>

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. **deleteAfterRun at-job manual run**: Job survived manual run, `enabled: true`, `deleteAfterRun: true`, `nextRunAtMs` preserved Ō£ģ
  2. **Recurring job manual run**: Job's `nextRunAtMs` preserved at natural cadence, NOT pushed forward by backoff Ō£ģ
  3. **Schema validation**: Both `cron list --json` and `cron show --json` returned `lastRunWasManual` field without validation errors Ō£ģ
  4. **New regression test**: `#83933: manual error on recurring job does not block scheduled fire via backoff` ŌĆö verifies `resolveJobErrorBackoffUntilMs()` returns `undefined` for manual errors, `isRunnableJob()` returns `true` within theoretical backoff window, `recomputeNextRunsForMaintenance()` computes natural cadence (not backoff floor), and control case confirms scheduled errors still apply backoff Ō£ģ

- **What was not tested**: Manual run with `status: "ok"` on recurring job (requires working LLM in Docker). Multi-node cluster, production load, actual delivery to chat channels.

target.title: test(qa): add mapped qa run profiles
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `pnpm openclaw qa run --profile smoke-ci|release` as the mapped QA profile entry point on top of the taxonomy mapping evidence map.
- Resolves profile/surface/category selectors to mapped scenario refs, converts them to QA suite scenario IDs, and dispatches through the existing `qa suite` runner.
- Preserves existing `qa run` self-check behavior when no mapped QA profile is provided, and preserves old root-profile parsing for non-mapped `qa run --profile <name>` values.
- Updates the public QA overview with mapped profile examples and adds `qa/AGENTS.md` plus the required `qa/CLAUDE.md` symlink for agent-ready QA mapping guidance.

Stacked on #91500.

target.title: fix(moonshot): rewrite duplicate native Kimi tool_call ids on replay
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix #51593 by enabling occurrence-aware replay ID sanitization for Moonshot `openai-completions` models.
- Preserve the first native Kimi ID such as `functions.read:0`, rewrite later duplicate occurrences to deterministic OpenAI-style `call_<24 hex>` IDs, and remap matching tool results in encounter order.
- Keep the transform idempotent and leave Moonshot responses-family replay behavior unchanged.
- Add the optional replay-policy field with a default-off contract, so Anthropic, OpenAI, and every provider that does not opt in retain existing behavior.
- Document the Moonshot replay behavior.

Closes #51593

## Verification

Local verification on the final PR head `5d160c5fd4c6217f5a6e8c5f71a697f802597052`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-call-id.test.ts src/plugins/provider-replay-helpers.test.ts src/agents/transcript-policy.policy.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-normalization.test.ts extensions/moonshot/index.test.ts` ŌĆö 70 passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts` ŌĆö 71 passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json` ŌĆö passed.
- `pnpm build` ŌĆö passed, including plugin SDK DTS/export checks and UI build.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p extensions/moonshot/tsconfig.json` ŌĆö passed.
- Focused oxlint, oxfmt, `git diff --check`, and `pnpm docs:list` ŌĆö passed.
- Fresh autoreview after the implementation: clean, confidence 0.95.
- Fresh autoreview after the CI lint correction: clean, confidence 0.99.

Live provider proof:

- Run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27456113649
- Moonshot job: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27456113649/job/81160985510
- Result: 8/8 Moonshot live tests passed.
- The validation-only test replayed two native `functions.noop:0` occurrences through Moonshot's actual replay policy, asserted that the first ID remained native and the duplicate became `call_<24 hex>` with paired tool-result IDs, then required live Kimi K2.6 to return a structured `noop` tool call. It passed in 1.067 seconds.

Final PR CI:

- https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27456110361 ŌĆö 117 jobs passed, no failures.

Known proof gap: the provider wire path was live-tested, but the original WhatsApp group-channel reproduction was not repeated. The fix is below the channel boundary in shared replay normalization.

## Risk

No config, migration, auth, or persisted-state change. Only repeated Moonshot chat-completions replay IDs change; first native IDs and responses-family behavior remain unchanged.

## Release note

Moonshot/Kimi replay history now rewrites duplicate native tool-call IDs to provider-safe OpenAI-style IDs, preventing duplicate-ID HTTP 400 failures in long or group-chat sessions.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" ŌĆö users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs ŌĆö gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by gating `onPartialReply` with `canCaptionFinalTts` (covers both always and tagged modes) instead of `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`.

### Media block preservation

When captioned final TTS is active, block suppression is narrowed to **text-only** payloads. Blocks containing media (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`), `presentation`, `interactive`, or `channelData` are delivered immediately with their text stripped (to avoid duplicating the caption). This applies to both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; narrow block suppression to text-only payloads (media blocks delivered with text stripped); `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression for tagged TTS on caption-capable channels, plain tagged block delivery, media block delivery with text stripped, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor; narrow `suppressBlockUserDelivery` to text-only blocks (media blocks delivered with text stripped) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts` | Regression tests: media block delivery with text stripped under `suppressBlockUserDelivery`, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/tools/tts.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability: opt-in criteria via `capabilities.tts.voice`, tagged mode behavior, text-only fallback, plugin API snippet |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability in channel plugin SDK guide: opt-in declaration, media block preservation, fallback behavior, tagged mode |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 Ō£ō 202 passed (202)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts
 Ō£ō 29 passed (29)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 Ō£ō 50 passed (50)

$ npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 Ō£ō 11 passed (11)

$ npx vitest run src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 Ō£ō 1 passed (1)

Total: 293 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode partial preview leaking raw `[[tts:text]]` directives, plus media block suppression during captioned final TTS.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `05b4483a8f`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix ŌĆö separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-before-fix.png)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note ŌĆö instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Built and installed from current head: `npm run build && npm pack && npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`
  2. Registered and restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway install && openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` and `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` ŌĆö sent plain messages and directive-bearing messages to `@thomas_fu_bot` on Telegram

- **Evidence after fix ŌĆö Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`) at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Always mode ŌĆö voice with caption, single message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-always-mode.png)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn, no separate text bubble.

- **Evidence ŌĆö Media block preservation with captioned final TTS at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Media block preserved ŌĆö image delivered + voice note with caption](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-media-block.png)

  When the bot generates an image during a TTS-enabled conversation (`auto: "always"`), the image is delivered immediately while text is deferred to the voice note caption. No media blocks are suppressed, and caption text does not duplicate as a separate text message.

- **Observed result after fix**: Always TTS auto mode delivers voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. Media blocks (images, files) are preserved and delivered immediately with text stripped. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no media loss.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested). Tagged mode live proof pending ŌĆö covered by unit tests (partial suppression + directive-only caption regression tests added).

Closes #83511

target.title: Docker image ships an extraneous stale openclaw in /app/node_modules (extensions pin the published release)
target.body:
## Summary
Remove the extraneous stale openclaw from the runtime Docker image. Extensions pin the published npm release in their package.json, causing `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` to fetch a second copy into `/app/node_modules/openclaw` with a `/app/node_modules/.bin/openclaw` shim. This is dead weight at rest and a footgun: any downstream image with `node_modules/.bin` on PATH silently runs the old published version against current gateway state.

Fixes #92551

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** The Dockerfile previously shipped a nested stale copy of openclaw inside node_modules via extension package.json pins. After this patch, the runtime image strips `/app/node_modules/openclaw` and `/app/node_modules/.bin/openclaw` after the COPY step. Module resolution from plugin dirs resolves `/app/dist` as before, so runtime behavior is unchanged.

**Real environment tested:** A local Docker build of the openclaw image, verified on the same host as the reporter's production environment.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
docker build -t openclaw:patched .
docker run --rm --entrypoint sh openclaw:patched -c '
  node -p "require(\"/app/node_modules/openclaw/package.json\").version" 2>&1 || echo "node_modules/openclaw removed"
  ls /app/node_modules/.bin/openclaw 2>&1 || echo "shim removed"
'
```

**Evidence after fix:**
```
node: cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'version')
node_modules/openclaw removed
ls: /app/node_modules/.bin/openclaw: No such file or directory
shim removed
```

**Observed result after fix:** The stale node_modules/openclaw and its .bin shim are gone. The image now contains only the current build at /app/dist and /app/openclaw.mjs. No behavioral change for runtime module resolution.

**What was not tested:** Full CI pipeline (pnpm check:changed), end-to-end Docker Compose deployment, and downstream image builds that may depend on the nested copy.

target.title: fix(opencode): restore Zen model catalog
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix classification: Root-cause provider catalog and routing fix for the bundled `opencode` Zen provider.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High ŌĆö the current PR head has fresh authenticated provider proof, family-specific live adapter proof, and focused regression coverage.
- Restore a plugin-owned OpenCode Zen model catalog for the bundled `opencode` provider.
- Add static rows for the 48 models currently advertised by Zen, plus runtime live discovery for `/zen/v1/models` when credentials are available.
- Restore dynamic model resolution, catalog augmentation, family-specific transport routing, and manifest metadata for manifest-first flows.

Fixes #92479

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The `opencode` Zen provider was migrated as an auth/setup surface without owning the Zen model catalog contract that `opencode-go` already supported. As a result, the provider registry had no source-of-truth model rows, no dynamic resolver for `opencode/<model>` references, and no family-aware transport mapping for Zen model IDs.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The patch restores the missing provider-owned catalog/resolver boundary instead of adding downstream string special cases. Runtime catalog loading now resolves real Zen rows when discovery succeeds, keeps a local seed available when discovery is unavailable, and maps each model family at the provider boundary before the generic LLM adapters run.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users who configure OpenCode Zen can select advertised Zen models directly instead of hand-registering every model or seeing `opencode/<model>` fail as unknown/unavailable.
- What did NOT change: This does not change non-OpenCode providers, does not change `opencode-go` runtime behavior, and does not expand the generic LLM adapter APIs outside this provider's catalog/routing contract.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: The provider plugin is the source-of-truth owner for bundled provider catalog metadata. `check-upstream-fixed` found main does not already cover this PR, and the remaining product decision is seed scope/ownership, not a competing implementation.
- Patch quality notes: The patch-quality warnings are expected for this provider-catalog repair: the static seed is a deliberate offline guard, default/undefined returns are catalog parsing boundaries, and the larger manifest diff is generated catalog metadata backed by live provider proof rather than unrelated refactor.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: The bundled `opencode` Zen provider exposed auth/setup but did not expose a model catalog or dynamic model resolver, so `opencode/<model>` selections failed as unknown or unavailable. This patch restores the provider-owned Zen catalog, keeps a 48-row seed visible when live discovery is unavailable, resolves shared `opencode-go` credentials for the Zen catalog, and routes representative Zen model families through the expected OpenClaw adapters.
- Real environment tested: Linux, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `e5497442`, authenticated OpenCode Zen key present but redacted. This machine requires `NODE_USE_ENV_PROXY=1` plus uppercase proxy variables for Node 22 outbound fetch/SDK traffic.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
source "$EVIDENCE_DIR/context.env"
OPENCODE_API_KEY=<redacted> \
  HTTPS_PROXY="$https_proxy" HTTP_PROXY="$http_proxy" ALL_PROXY="$all_proxy" NO_PROXY="$no_proxy" \
  NODE_USE_ENV_PROXY=1 \
  corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir "$TASK_WORKTREE" exec tsx \
  "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-zen-live-proof.mts" > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-zen-live-proof.log" 2>&1

corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir "$TASK_WORKTREE" exec vitest run extensions/opencode/index.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix: Authenticated discovery returned the 48-row Zen catalog with an Authorization header present, the provider catalog resolved using shared `opencode-go` credentials, and representative live completions returned `ok` through the affected OpenClaw adapter families.

```text
OpenCode Zen live proof at 2026-06-13T03:27:09.724Z
worktree=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-92495
credential=present (redacted)
node_env_proxy=enabled
authenticated_discovery_models=48
authenticated_discovery_request url=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models authorization_header=present status=200
shared_opencode_go_credential_catalog_models=48
live_request family=Claude model=claude-haiku-4-5 api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen payload_model=claude-haiku-4-5 stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=GPT model=gpt-5-nano api=openai-responses baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 payload_model=gpt-5-nano stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Gemini model=gemini-3-flash api=google-generative-ai baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 payload_model=gemini-3-flash stream=undefined max_tokens=undefined stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Qwen model=qwen3.6-plus api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen payload_model=qwen3.6-plus stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request_attempt family=MiniMax model=minimax-m2.7 api=openai-completions baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 result=failed error="unexpected text: <empty>; content="
live_request family=MiniMax model=minimax-m2.5 api=openai-completions baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 payload_model=minimax-m2.5 stream=true max_tokens=64 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
result=passed
```

- Observed result after fix: `opencode` now exposes a 48-model Zen catalog, can populate the catalog from live discovery with a redacted real credential, accepts the shared OpenCode credential path from `opencode-go`, and successfully completes real provider calls through Claude/Anthropic messages, GPT/OpenAI responses, Gemini/Google generative AI, Qwen/Anthropic messages, and MiniMax/OpenAI chat completions routes.
- What was not tested: I did not live-call all 48 Zen models individually. The authenticated discovery proof used the provider-catalog live helper with a direct fetch guard because this local machine's default SSRF-guarded fetch path times out behind the configured proxy; the production no-discovery path remains covered by static catalog tests, and the real model requests above exercised the actual OpenClaw runtime provider adapters. `minimax-m2.7` returned an empty stream body in this account/environment, so the MiniMax family proof used `minimax-m2.5`, which returned `ok` through the same `openai-completions` route.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target test file: `extensions/opencode/index.test.ts` plus live evidence script `opencode-zen-live-proof.mts` stored outside the repo evidence directory.
- Scenario locked in: Regression tests assert the Zen catalog exposes the 48 model rows, dynamic model resolution works for Zen IDs, shared `opencode-go` credentials populate the `opencode` catalog path, and Claude/GPT/Gemini/Qwen/MiniMax families route to the expected adapter/base URL pairs.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: A focused provider test locks the source-of-truth catalog/routing contract without adding broad adapter mocks, while the live proof covers the external provider behavior that unit tests cannot prove.

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-92495

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  10 passed (10)
Duration  44.99s
```

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 ŌĆö authenticated discovery and representative live requests:** Addressed by the redacted authenticated `/zen/v1/models` request (`authorization_header=present status=200`) and live `ok` completions through Claude, GPT, Gemini, Qwen, and MiniMax adapter families.
- **RF-002 ŌĆö stronger real behavior proof:** Addressed by the authenticated external Zen proof above; this is no longer limited to public discovery, static catalog listing, or unit tests.
- **RF-003 ŌĆö shared credentials and affected adapters:** Addressed by `shared_opencode_go_credential_catalog_models=48` plus live requests through `anthropic-messages`, `openai-responses`, `google-generative-ai`, and `openai-completions`.
- **RF-004 ŌĆö static catalog ownership/synchronization risk:** The 48-row seed is intentionally provider-owned so the Zen provider remains usable when live discovery is unavailable. Live discovery is still preferred whenever credentials are present; if the provider owner wants a smaller seed or different ownership policy, that is a product/maintenance decision rather than a code defect in this patch.
- **RF-005 ŌĆö routing defect and maintainer decision boundary:** The routing defect is covered by tests and live requests. The remaining maintainer decision is the acceptable seed scope for Zen, not whether the restored catalog/routing behavior works.

## Merge risk

- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: ­¤Ü© compatibility`, `merge-risk: ­¤Ü© auth-provider`, provider catalog/routing, generated manifest metadata, and large-diff catalog seed review cost.
- Risk explanation: The compatibility risk is that a provider-owned seed can drift from Zen if the upstream catalog changes before live discovery runs. The auth-provider risk is bounded to resolving the same OpenCode credential across `opencode` and `opencode-go`; no key is stored in the repo or PR body, and live proof used a redacted environment variable only. The large diff is mostly generated manifest/catalog rows rather than a cross-cutting runtime refactor.
- Why acceptable: The previous behavior left Zen effectively unusable without hand registration. The patch restores the provider contract at its owner boundary, keeps live discovery as the preferred source when credentials are present, preserves a safe no-credential/no-discovery local seed, and now has real provider proof across the affected adapter families.
